title: Capitola Road - March 23

communications.subject: California Public Records Act Request: Capitola Road - March 23
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the California Public Records Act, I hereby request the following records:

I am stating the purpose of this request so that you may assist me as required by California Government Code (“Gov”) § 7922.600(a)(1). The purpose of this California Public Records Act request is to determine the existence or non-existence of a specific record.

Please note that I am not requesting the record itself, only confirmation of its existence or non-existence. Thus, Civil Code § 1798.90.55, law enforcement exemptions, the public interest exemption or any other laws or exemptions do not apply to, and are not relevant to, this request.

Please confirm the existence or non-existence of the following Flock Safety ALPR footage record:

License Plate: WCD6448 Texas
Date: March 23, 2026
Time Frame: 11:40 AM - 11:50 AM
Camera Location: In the median of Capitola Road near cross street Lotman Drive  
Camera Lat/long: 36.97371295023479, -121.97173395944117

Please keep in mind your agency’s obligations under the law require you to:

- Respond within 10 calendar days.

- Help me to make a focused and effective request by (1) identifying records and information that are responsive, (2) describing the information technology and physical location in which the records exist and (3) providing suggestions for overcoming any practical issues.

- Broadly construe all provisions that further the public’s right of access, and apply any limitations on access as narrowly as possible, per Article 1, Section 3(b)(2) of the California Constitution.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 10 calendar days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

David Moore

communications.subject: Public Records Act Response - PRR 26-103, 104, 105, and 106
communications.communication:
Re: March 26, 2025 Public Records Act Request (PRR # 26-103, 104, 105, 106)

Dear Requestor,

The City of Capitola ("City") is in receipt of the four Public Records Act requests you submitted, received on March 26, 2026 and March 31, 2026 ("Requests").  For simplicity, the City is treating the Requests as one and is responding to your Requests with this letter. A copy of your Requests are attached.

The City intends to cooperate to the fullest extent possible with the Public Records Act ("PRA") (Government Code section 7920.000 et seq.). Under established California law, the City is obliged to comply with a request for a public record so long as the requestor makes a specific and focused request for information, the City maintains that information in its ordinary course of business, the information is disclosable, and the record can be located with reasonable effort.  The PRA provides for the inspection or copying of existing identifiable public records; it does not compel the City to respond directly to questions or create new records, lists, or reports in response to a request.

You state that the purpose of your Requests is to "determine the existence or non-existence of a specific record."  However, the PRA is for copying or inspecting public records.  The City is not required to respond to requests for information, including confirmation as to whether records exist.  If you would like to copy or inspect a record, please make a specific and focused request for the record you seek.

With this letter, the City considers your Requests closed.  If you have any questions regarding the City's response or would like to discuss your Request, please contact me at thernandez@ci.capitola.ca.us<mailto:thernandez@ci.capitola.ca.us>.

Sincerely,

Tracie Hernandez
Police Records Manager


[cid:image001.png@01DCC5EB.985F2170]
Tracie Hernandez
Police Records & Evidence Manager
Phone: 831-475-4242 x202
Email: thernandez@ci.capitola.ca.us<mailto:thernandez@ci.capitola.ca.us>
Address: 422 Capitola Avenue
Website: www.cityofcapitola.org<http://www.cityofcapitola.org/>
Instagram: Capitola Police Department<https://www.instagram.com/capitolapolice/?hl=en>
Facebook: Capitola Police Department<https://www.facebook.com/CapitolaPoliceDepartment>
**CONFIDENTIAL:** This communication contains confidential information intended solely for the designated recipient(s). Unauthorized interception, review, use, or disclosure of this information is prohibited and may violate laws, including but not limited to the Electronic Communications Privacy Act. If you are not the intended recipient of this message, please note that any dissemination, distribution, or copying of this communication is strictly prohibited. We kindly ask you to contact the sender and delete all copies of this message.

Any opinions, conclusions, or information in this message that are not related to our official business should not be considered endorsed by the sender.

title: Library Contract Request: HeinOnline (University Of Hawaii, West Oahu)

communications.subject: Hawaii Uniform Information Practices Act Request: Library Contract Request: HeinOnline (University Of Hawaii, West Oahu)
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Hawaii Uniform Information Practices Act, I hereby request the following records:

1) The most recent contract, governing, base, or master license agreement with William S. Hein & Co. relating to the licensing of the “HeinOnline” database;
2) All related terms and conditions, terms of service, amendments, addenda, appendices, attachments, product terms, MOUs, MOAs, data processing agreements, and/or letters of intent; and
3) All financial documents or records of financial transactions, exchanges or obligations related to the contractual relations with William S. Hein & Co. for the “HeinOnline” database. 

Please do not limit your search to strictly direct agreements or renewals between the institution and William S. Hein & Co., but include any agreements and related documentation through consortia such as the Hawai’i Library Consortium, the Greater Western Library Alliance (GWLA), or Lyrasis.

Responsive materials include all contracts, agreements, amendments, appendices, invoices, and any other documents listed above that incorporate language governing terms of use of the “HeinOnline” database.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 10 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Ellen Dubinsky

communications.subject: UIPA
communications.communication:
Hello Ms. Dubinsky,

First off, deepest apologies for this tardy submission that was delayed due
to our campus mailroom not knowing where to send mail addressed to the UIPA
Office.   We don't have an UIPA Office, so notices were put to the side or
forwarded to another office who didn't know what the UIPA Office was
either.

I finally got the 3rd and 4th notice on the same day earlier this month.
Please feel free to send all future requests to the University of Hawaii
West Oahu Chancellor's Office.

Attaching our response to your request.

Please feel free to contact me if you have any questions.

Thank you,
*Chris*

Christine Neves
University of Hawaii - West Oahu
Office of the Chancellor
Phone:  808 689-2773

title: James Pinjuv Email Metadata

communications.subject: The Missouri Sunshine Law Request: James Pinjuv Email Metadata
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the The Missouri Sunshine Law, I hereby request the following records:

Metadata for all email communications which included "jimmerpinjuv@gmail.com" as either the sender and/or receiver, including being CCed or BCCed, between 01/01/2025 and the date this request is processed.

The specific metadata data points I'm interested in are: to address(es), from address, cc address(es), bcc address(es), sent date/time, received date/time, subject line, and names of all attachments. This request does not seek the contents of the emails themselves, and only the specified email metadata. Please do not include duplicate emails. Please provide me with the records in an Excel format.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 3 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Michael

title: CLERK CORRESPONDENCE TEMPLATES / STANDARD LANGUAGE

communications.subject: Wisconsin Open Records Act Request: CLERK CORRESPONDENCE TEMPLATES / STANDARD LANGUAGE
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Wisconsin Open Records Act, I hereby request the following records:

This is a request under the Wisconsin Open Records Law, Wis. Stat. §§ 19.31–19.39.

I request copies of any form letters, templates, standardized correspondence, or model language used or recommended for clerk communications regarding:

• “pay the filing fee or show good cause” notices,
• conditional fee-waiver communications, or
• correspondence addressing filing-fee obligations following appellate procedural changes (including appeal-to-writ conversion).

This request seeks administrative records only and does not request records concerning judicial reasoning or case-specific adjudication.

If responsive records exist in multiple versions or revisions, please provide the most current version and any prior versions still in use during the past five years.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 10 business days.

Sincerely,

Alexander Lennon

title: Leadership and constituent emails (Dover School District/SAU11)

communications.subject: New Hampshire Right to Know Law Request: Leadership and constituent emails (Dover School District/SAU11)
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the New Hampshire Right to Know Law, I hereby request the following records:

A copy of all email communications sent and received by each school board chair in the district/SAU related to their position between Jan 1, 2026 and Jan 31, 2026, including but not limited to emails with constituents. This request is inclusive of all email sent to their official email, and all correspondence related to their elected position sent or received on district or secondary accounts.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

In some cases, these records may be considered exempt from disclosure because of a privacy interest or some other concern. When considering whether a government record should be released, the courts routinely conduct a balancing test to weigh the public's interest in the records versus the government's interest in non-disclosure. We believe the public interest in these specific records is very high.

Nothing prevents you from making these records available to the public. Both the spirit and letter of the state's Right-to-Know law ensure "the greatest possible public access" to government records and proceedings. We ask that you consider this request carefully and conduct your own balancing test if you are inclined to deny any portion of this request.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 5 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Audrey Nielsen (Muckrock) and Jonathan Van Fleet (Concord Monitor)

title: State of Delaware Salaries 2025

communications.subject: Delaware Freedom of Information Act Request: State of Delaware Salaries 2025
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Delaware Freedom of Information Act, I hereby request the following records:

State of Delaware 2025 compensation data for all employees.
Please provide the following fields for each employee in the dataset:

Employee # | Last Name | First Name | Middle Name/Initial | Race | Gender | Agency | Department | Job Title | Compensation (base pay, hourly rate, total pay, etc.) | Overtime | Benefits Pay | Date Hired | Date Terminated | Full-time/Part-time | Location of Work City/State

Note: If not all fields listed above are releasable as public record or available please disregard those individual fields for this request.
I would prefer the request be filled electronically, by either e-mail attachment or FTP Download if available, otherwise sent through mail via CD/DVD. I would appreciate the request to be filled in an accessible format, including but not limited to: CSV, Excel, Microsoft Access database, or other bulk list file type.
The requested data will be made available to the public free of charge and will not be used for commercial solicitation purposes. In the event that fees for personnel time and materials can not be waived, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 15 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Jonathan Rudenberg, a citizen of Delaware, in coordination with Tyler King.

[Redacted]

communications.subject: RE: FOIA Request from Jonathan Rudenberg
communications.communication:
Thank you for submitting a Freedom of Information Act (FOIA) request to the State of Delaware's Office of Management and Budget. The provisions of 29 Del. C. § 10003(a) provide for "citizen" access to public records. Upon review of your request, it has been determined that you are not a citizen of the State of Delaware and therefore the request is denied. OMB now considers this FOIA request closed.

FOIA Coordinator, OMB

communications.subject: RE: Delaware Freedom of Information Act Request: State of Delaware Salaries 2025
communications.communication:
Good morning, 

As stated previously, there is not a section on the FOIA form to manually enter information confirming citizenship, as requested. There is a section to provide a preferred mailing address. As you know, hopefully, an individual may have a separate preferred mailing address than their residential address. 

Aside from manually entering the residential information in an email reply to the denial based on residency (which is not equivalent to preferred mailing address), what other "manual entry" options are present on the FOIA form present on the State's website? As stated in our last message, it doesn't appear to have an area for this, however, we are open to any available remedies for this issue that don't seem immediately present. 

I eagerly await your response.

communications.subject: RE: Delaware Freedom of Information Act Request: State of Delaware Salaries 2025
communications.communication: The  “Mailing Address” section does not ask for a preferred mailing address, it seeks [the] mailing address of the requesting party submitting the request.

Best Regards

FOIA Coordinator OMB

title: Untitled

communications.subject: Freedom of Information Act Request: Untitled
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Freedom of Information Act, I hereby request the following records:

Daily snapshots of the Motor Carrier Census file (also known as Company Census or MCS-150 data) for the period January 1, 2000  through January 27, 2026.   If daily snapshots are not available, I would be interested in monthly or quarterly snapshots.  

Fields of interest (standard census fields):                                                                                                       
  - DOT_NUMBER                                                                                                                                       
  - LEGAL_NAME, DBA_NAME                                                                                                                             
  - PHY_STREET, PHY_CITY, PHY_STATE, PHY_ZIP                                                                                                         
  - NBR_POWER_UNIT (power units)                                                                                                                     
  - DRIVER_TOTAL (drivers)                                                                                                                           
  - MCS150_MILEAGE, MCS150_MILEAGE_YEAR                                                                                                              
  - CARRIER_OPERATION (interstate/intrastate)                                                                                                        
  - CARGO types (CRGO_* fields)                                                                                                                      
  - Authority status fields                                                                                                                          
  - MCS150_DATE (filing date)                                                                                                                        

Preferred format: CSV, pipe-delimited text, or any machine-readable format.                                                                        

Please note that this request has been made numerous times per a review of FOIA logs, specifically FMCS-2023-05358, FMCS-2023-03393, FMCS-2023-03511 in FY2023, and also in previous years.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 20 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Ian White

communications.subject: FW: Freedom of Information Act Request: Untitled
communications.communication:
Over the past year, FMCSA has improved the sharing of data with the public through the DOT - Public Data Portal<https://data.transportation.gov/> (Datahub).  By selecting "Trucking & Motorcoaches" you can search our many datasets.  You can use the search catalog in the upper right corner to type in key words like "Carrier" to see what kind of data we share with the public.   You are also able to search Company Census File which contains data for active, inactive an pending entities registered with FMCSA.

The FOIA provides that records made available under the FOIA's proactive disclosure provisions of subsection (a)(1) and (a)(2) are not themselves subject to individualized FOIA requests, see 5 U.S.C. § 552(a)(3)(A). This information is already made publicly available through the FMCSA Datahub.

After reviewing the data we share with the public, please let us know if there is something you are unable to find.

Thank you
FMCSA FOIA Office

communications.subject: RE: Freedom of Information Act Request: Untitled
communications.communication:
Thank you for your response. I wish to significantly narrow this request. Data I seek is daily snapshots of Motor Carrier Census file (also known as Company Census or MCS-150 data) for the period December 2023 through January 2026. I seek this data in CSV, pipe-delimited text, or any machine-readable format.

Please note this archived data is NOT published by the FMCSA, DOT or any third party. I have searched on the Public Data Portal and it is not available. This is data which was once published, and taken offline after it was replaced by a more current version.

Thank you and please let me know if you have questions.

Jennifer

After speaking with our data office, our new Datahub has taken over the dissemination of census data files to the public.  The Datahub is updated daily and effectively maintains daily snapshots.  We are able to provide year‑end (December) snapshots for 2023, 2024, and 2025.

Please let me know if you want to proceed with this information.

Thank you
Jennifer

Jennifer Weatherly
Director, Office of Information Services
Federal Motor Carrier Safety Administration
1200 New Jersey Avenue, SE | Washington, DC 20590
M: (202) 420-0106
Jennifer.Weatherly@dot.gov<mailto:Jennifer.Weatherly@dot.gov>

communications.subject: RE: Freedom of Information Act Request: Untitled
communications.communication: Thank you, I would like snapshots for Dec 2023, Dec 2024 and Dec 2025 per my request. Please let me know if you have any questions.

communications.subject: Re: Freedom of Information Act Request: Untitled
communications.communication:
Good afternoon,
In response to your request for end of year snapshot data, please see the
links below.  End of year data for 2025 is currently available on the
Datahub for public access.

Please note that these files are too large for standard applications like
Excel or Notepad, as each contains between 1.5 million records.  To view
these files, you will need data analysis tools such as Python, R, or SAS or
a specialized large-file text editor.

FMCSA_Census_2023: https://public.huddle.com/b/wWEZMl/index.html

FMCSA_Census_2024: https://public.huddle.com/b/oDkWNY/index.html

Please let me know if you have any questions.

Thank you
-- 

Jennifer Weatherly

Director, Office of Information Services

Federal Motor Carrier Safety Administration

1200 New Jersey Avenue, SE | Washington, DC 20590

M: (202) 420-0106

Jennifer.Weatherly@dot.gov

communications.subject: RE: Freedom of Information Act Request: Untitled
communications.communication:
Hi Jennifer

Thank you for your response.  Thank you for providing the two census snapshot files (CENSUS_FILE_231229 and CENSUS_FILE_20241227). We reviewed both files against the publicly available SODA Company Census File (data.transportation.gov, dataset az4n-8mr2) and found four issues that prevent us from using  the snapshots as delivered.

- Issue 1: Power unit counts are classification codes, not integers. The TOT_PWR field contains letter codes (A through Z) for 54% of rows  instead of numeric power unit counts. The SODA Company Census File provides clean integer values for the same carriers in the  NBR_POWER_UNIT field. Large carriers such as UPS (DOT 21800) and Walmart (DOT 63585) return empty or coded values in the delivered files  but show 28,307 and 13,618 power units respectively in the SODA table. We need the actual integer count.

- Issue 2: Mileage year is a date string, not a year. The MLG150_YEAR field contains date-formatted strings (eg "01-JUN-74") for 100% of  rows. The SODA table provides this as a 4-digit year integer (eg 2023). We need a 4-digit year matching the SODA field  MCS150_MILEAGE_YEAR.

- Issue 3: Mileage is missing for 17% of rows. The MLG150 field is empty for carriers that have populated mileage values in the SODA table,  including large national carriers. We need MLG150 populated for all carriers that have mileage in the SODA sms_input_census table.

- Issue 4: Header row truncated at 1024 bytes. The tab-delimited header is cut at exactly 1024 bytes, splitting the column name "TRMHMTRUCK"  across two lines. The file has 163 data columns but the header shows 93 on the first line and 71 on the second. We need the header  exported with no line-length limit.

- December 2025 snapshot. Your office indicated that a December 2025 snapshot is available on the DOT DataHub. We checked and this is not  the case. The Company Census File on DataHub (dataset az4n-8mr2) is a live, daily-updating dataset that reflects the current state of the  census as of today. It is not a historical snapshot archive. There is no December 2025 point-in-time file available on that platform.

The delivered files do contain usable data for DOT numbers, legal names, states, driver counts and operating status. The power unit and  mileage fields are the two we cannot use in their current format.

Thank you again for your diligence and support, I really appreciate it.

communications.subject: Re: Freedom of Information Act Request: Untitled
communications.communication:
Good morning,
Below are the updated links and a graphic detailing the upload directions
for the 2023–2025 extracts. Please note that we will not create further
extracts after 2025. Moving forward, the Datahub can be used to create
snapshots as needed.  Please let me know if you have any trouble with
accessing this data.  Thank you.


   - Link to 2023 Data: https://public.huddle.com/b/NLjlXB/index.html
   - Link to 2024 Data: https://public.huddle.com/b/ZqezOz/index.html
   - Link to 2025 Data: https://public.huddle.com/b/aEpwLG/index.html


[image: image.png]
-- 

Jennifer Weatherly

Director, Office of Information Services

Federal Motor Carrier Safety Administration

1200 New Jersey Avenue, SE | Washington, DC 20590

M: (202) 420-0106

Jennifer.Weatherly@dot.gov

title: Wayzata Police Department, MN - Flock Audit (Condensed)

communications.subject: Minnesota Government Data Practices Act Request: Wayzata Police Department, MN - Flock Audit (Condensed)
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Minnesota Government Data Practices Act, I hereby request the following records:

1) User account audit logs documenting each time a department account has conducted a search or taken another action on the department's Flock Safety ALPR system from 1/1/2025 until the date this request is processed. Flock audit logs are retained indefinitely and contain metadata including, but not limited to: the date of the search, the time of the search, the search term/rules, and the user conducting the search. The audit logs are recorded in a database so I ask that you provide it to me as a .csv, .xls, or other database filetype.
--
2) Audit logs documenting the access reason for each time a department account has conducted a search on the department's Flock Safety ALPR system from 1/1/2025 until the date this request is processed. The access policy of Wayzata PD for the Flock Safety ALPR system requires a "valid reason". If this valid reason is not recorded anywhere, please indicate as such. If there are audit logs with information regarding ongoing cases and therefore they cannot be provided, please indicate the count of the logs not provided for that reason, and provide the remaining logs that do not have information regarding ongoing cases. Otherwise, if logs contain information that cannot be publicly shared, please redact the information, and provide a reason for the redaction.
--
3) Audit logs for any other departments who ran queries against Wayzata PD's Flock Data.
--
4) The names of all the agencies with which data is shared with through the Flock Safety ALPR system from 1/1/2025 until the date this request is processed
--
5) If any exist, please provide all agreements will local homeowner associations or private business or individuals for access to Flock Safety systems.
--
6) Correspondence (emails, letters, memoranda) between SFPD personnel and Flock personnel relating to the purchase, installation, or usage of the ALPRs. Flock's email domain is "flocksafety.com". You may de-duplicate emails as needed in case Flock sends out newsletters or emails to multiple Wayzata PD members. Please search back one year from the date of completion of this request. 

In the event that you determine certain records or portions of records are exempt from disclosure, I ask that you provide an explanation of the records or portions of records being withheld and the statutory exemptions under which they are being withheld. I have already reviewed the Flock Transparency Page for Wayzata Police: https://transparency.flocksafety.com/wayzata-mn-pd

I am requesting these records as a journalist in order to write articles in the public interest.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 10 business days.

Sincerely,

Alan J. Segal

title: Suffolk County police disciplinary files

communications.subject: Massachusetts Public Records Law Request: Suffolk County police disciplinary files
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Massachusetts Public Records Law, I hereby request the following records:

- Any and all lists, spreadsheets or other organized datasets that detail potential or substantiated allegations of misconduct and/or impeached or impeachable testimony, created under Brady v. Maryland and/or Giglio v. United States.
- Any and all records regarding substantiated allegations of misconduct or violations of internal department policy or criminal law by specific law enforcement officers.

I also request that, if appropriate, fees be waived as we believe this request is in the public interest, as suggested but not stipulated by the recommendations of the Massachusetts Supervisor of Public Records. The requested documents will be made available to the general public free of charge as part of the public information service at MuckRock.com, and is not made for commercial usage.

I expect the request to be filled in an accessible format, including for screen readers, which provide text-to-speech for persons unable to read print. Files that are not accessible to screen readers include, for example, .pdf image files as well as physical documents.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 10 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Dillon Bergin

title: Internal Complaints (2010 - Present) (Skagit County Sheriff (washington))

communications.subject: Washington Public Records Act Request: Internal Complaints (2010 - Present) (Skagit County Sheriff (washington))
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Washington Public Records Act, I hereby request the following records:

For the period from January 1, 2010 to the date this request was filed, please provide a spreadsheet of all complaints against officers in CSV or Excel format with only the following columns: Investigation/Complaint No., Incident Type, Occurred Date, Complaint Reported On Date, Employee Badge Number, Employee First Name, Employee Last Name, Allegation, Allegation: Policy/Directive Violated, Allegation: Finding, Disposition, Discipline Imposed.

Please provide all complaints, regardless of severity,in cluding any complaints that may not have been classified for full investigation and were referred to an employee's supervisor for counseling or retraining as well as complaints that were investigated by an employee's command instead of the normal internal affairs process. I would ask that you fulfill my request in a single installment in one spreadsheet instead of splitting it up into multiple annual spreadsheets if possible.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 5 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Rose Terse

communications.subject: Your Skagit County, WA public records request #26-716 has been submitted.
communications.communication:
Skagit County, WA ************************************************************************ Your record request #26-716 has been submitted successfully. Thank you for submitting your request to Skagit County Records Department. Someone from our records team will respond as required by RCW 42.56.520, within five business days. ln calculating the five business days, the following are not counted: the day the county receives the request, Saturdays, Sundays and holidays. RCW 1.12.040. See also WAC 44-14-03006. lf the request is received by the County after County business hours. the request shall be deemed to have been received by the county on the next business day. A response may be (a) providing the records, (b) providing a link to records available online, (c) providing a reasonable estimated date of when the records will be available, (d) requesting clarification, or (e) denial of the request. If you have any questions or concerns, please contact me at 360-416-1152, or by email at pherman@co.skagit.wa.us. Sincerely, Pamela Herman Certified Public Records Officer, Central Services Skagit County 360-416-1152 Public Records Act (PRA) Notice. Any information, including certain personal information, submitted to the County by e-mail or other method may be subject to disclosure as a public record under RCW chapter 42.56.  ************************************************************************ Reply to this email or sign in to contact Skagit County, WA.

Reports & All Other Requests for Information Includes copies of reports, collisions, background checks, parking citations, and any other protected, non-public record. *** As of March 2026, due to a high volume of requests, there is a significant delay in processing time. *** * After submitting your request, please allow 2 weeks before following up with our team and 4-6 weeks for processing & delivery of requested records. * Do not duplicate inquiries by requesting the same record(s) via different submission types (mail, email, fax, in-person, etc.). communications.subject: [External Message Added] Santa Barbara Police Department public records request #26-216
communications.communication:
Santa Barbara Police Department ************************************************************************ A message was sent to you regarding record request #26-216: Per California Government Code §7922.535, agencies may take an additional 14 days to determine whether disclosable records exist for any of the following reasons: (1) The need to search for and collect the requested records from field facilities or other establishments that are separate from the office processing the request. (2) The need to search for, collect, and appropriately examine a voluminous amount of separate and distinct records that are demanded in a single request. (3) The need for consultation, which shall be conducted with all practicable speed, with another agency having substantial interest in the determination of the request or among two or more components of the agency having substantial subject matter interest therein. (4) The need to compile data, to write programming language or a computer program, or to construct a computer report to extract data. In order to determine whether disclosable records in our custody exist, the City requires the additional 14 days to respond as provided by the Government Code above and in compliance with that code, you will receive a response regarding your request no later than April 3, 2026. ************************************************************************ Questions about your request? Reply to this email or sign in to contact staff at Santa Barbara Police Department.

title: Request to Army for all reports on entertainment industry interaction/collaboration

communications.subject: Freedom of Information Act Request: Request to Army for all reports on entertainment industry interaction/collaboration
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Freedom of Information Act, I hereby request the following records:

All Entertainment Liaison Office (ELO) reports, "Entertainment Media Weekly" reports, or other reports pertaining to interactions or collaborations between the US Army and entertainment industry entities, inclusive of all years for which records are still retained by the Army. I request a search of all locations and databases where such records may be found, including but not limited to public affairs offices.  

I am the Director of Research Operations at a 501(c)(3) non-profit organization called The Decoding Culture Foundation. These documents will be used for public education and research purposes. I therefore request a waiver of all fees that may otherwise apply. Attached is a document with organization letterhead confirming my position, as well as the organization's 501(c)(3) application and approval form.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 20 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Brett Carollo

communications.subject: Fw: FOIA Request Generated from RMDA Website
communications.communication:
Greetings

You have reached the Headquarters Department of Army Freedom of Information Act (FOIA) Referral Center.

Our office is not the records owner of the documents that you are seeking. Requests for information and documents under the purview of the Army are referred to the proponent agency for appropriate handling in accordance with Army Regulation 25-55, The Army Freedom of Information Act program. Your FOIA request has been assigned a case tracking number: FA-25-1467 /FP-25-014197.

The correct office to process your request is Office of the Chief of Public Affairs. Your request was referred to that office via email for processing and direct response to you.

Note: Once the appropriate office receives your request, a new FOIA case tracking number will be assigned, and their contact information is listed below.

Office of the Chief of Public Affairs
1500 Army Pentagon
Room 1E487
Washington DC 20310
Phone: (703) 693-6578
Email: Usarmy.pentagon.hqda-ocpa.mbx.foia@army.mil

Thank you for your submission.
DA FOIA

communications.subject: [External Message Added] County of Napa public records request #26-245
communications.communication:
County of Napa ************************************************************************ A message was sent to you regarding record request #26-245: Napa County received your Public Records Act request. Govt. Code section 7922.535 provides in part as follows: “Each agency, upon a request for a copy of records, shall, within 10 days from receipt of the request, determine whether the request, in whole or in part, seeks copies of disclosable public records in the possession of the agency and shall promptly notify the person making the request of the determination and the reasons therefor ... In unusual circumstances, the time limit prescribed in this article ... may be extended by written notice … to the person making the request, setting forth the reasons for the extension and the date on which a determination is expected to be dispatched. No notice shall specify a date that would result in an extension for more than 14 days.” This response therefore serves as written notice of the need for an extension of time for your request. The reasons for the extension include * The need to search for and collect the requested records from field facilities or other establishments that are separate from the office processing the request. (Govt. Code section 7922.535(c)(1).) * The need to search for, collect, and appropriately examine a voluminous amount of separate and distinct records that are demanded in a single request. (Govt. Code section 7922.535(c)(2).) * The need for consultation, which shall be conducted with all practicable speed, with another agency having substantial interest in the determination of the request or among two or more components of the agency having substantial subject matter interest therein. (Govt. Code section 7922.535(c)(3).) * The need to compile data, to write programming language or a computer program, or to construct a computer report to extract data. (Govt. Code section 7922.535(c)(4).) We have initiated the processing of your request to identify records and determine if any records are exempt from disclosure pursuant to Govt. Code section 7927.500. We aim to have a determination dispatched no later than 3/27/26, which will inform you of the anticipated date when any disclosable records will be made available to you, the copying charges if physical copies are desired, and the explanation for not disclosing any exempt records. Should you have any questions, please reply to this email. ************************************************************************ Questions about your request? Reply to this email or sign in to contact staff at County of Napa.

Technical support: See our help page

County of Napa ************************************************************************ A message was sent to you regarding record request #26-245: A report with all data that is releasable is attached however names, DOB, and actual reports are not releasable because such information, taken as a whole, could comprise criminal history records. Criminal history records of an individual constitute local summary criminal history information per Penal Code Section 13300 et seq. and under the reasoning of Westbrook v. County of Los Angeles (1994) 27 Cal.App.4th 157 and 89 Ops.Cal.Atty.Gen. 204 (2006) and therefore are not subject to release under the Public Records Act. (See also Government Code Section 7927.705.) Additionally, no MOU outlined in section 5 exists. Thank you. ************************************************************************ Questions about your request? Reply to this email or sign in to contact staff at County of Napa.

communications.subject: Freedom of Information Act Request: Navy support for Netflix series "The Recruit"
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Freedom of Information Act, I hereby request the following records:

All documents pertaining to collaboration between the US Navy and the production of the Netflix TV series "The Recruit" (2022-2025), season 2. Such documents may include but should not be limited to: script notes; production assistance agreements; photographs; and all records of communications between employees of the Navy and members of the cast, crew, and production company of "The Recruit," including memos, notes of meetings and conference calls, emails, and other forms of communication.

"The Recruit," season 2, appears in a list of Navy-supported productions for the year 2024 that was released to me through FOIA. That list is attached to this request.

I am the Director of Research Operations at a 501(c)(3) non-profit organization called The Decoding Culture Foundation as well as the editor-in-chief and a contributor to a research magazine published by said foundation titled "Cultural Engineering Studies." These documents will be used for public education and research purposes. I therefore request a waiver of all fees that may otherwise apply. Attached is a document with organization letterhead confirming my position, as well as the organization's 501(c)(3) application and approval form.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 20 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Brett Carollo

title: Water Quality Complaints in Albany, Schenectady, and Troy MSA (Niskayuna Consolidated Water District)

communications.subject: New York Freedom of Information Law Request: Water Quality Complaints in Albany, Schenectady, and Troy MSA (Niskayuna Consolidated Water District)
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the New York Freedom of Information Law, I hereby request the following records:

I am requesting access to and copies of records related to water quality complaints and issues within the Albany, Schenectady, and Troy Metropolitan Statistical Area (MSA) for the period from January 1, 2018, to the present.

Specifically, I am seeking the following information:

1) The total number of water quality complaints received by your agency/authority/utility, broken down by year.

2) A detailed summary of each water quality complaint, including:
2a. The date of the complaint
2b. The location (address or nearest cross streets) of the complaint
2c. The nature of the complaint (e.g., odor, taste, discoloration, contamination concerns)
2d. Any actions taken by your agency/authority/utility to address the complaint
2e. The resolution or current status of the complaint

3) Any reports, memos, or other documentation related to water quality complaints, investigations, and resolutions within the specified area and timeframe.

If any of this information is available in electronic format, I kindly request that you provide it via email. If physical copies are necessary, please inform me of any copying or postage fees in advance, and I will make arrangements for payment.

If any portion of this request is denied, please provide a detailed explanation for the denial and inform me of any appeal procedures available under New York State law.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 5 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Jackson Parker

communications.subject: RE: New York Freedom of Information Law Request: Water Quality Complaints in Albany, Schenectady, and Troy MSA (Niskayuna Consolidated Water District)
communications.communication:
Subject: FOIL Appeal for Denial of Access - Water Quality Complaints in Albany, Schenectady, and Troy MSA (Niskayuna Consolidated Water District)

Dear Records Access Officer,

I am writing to appeal the summary denial of access to records that I requested under the New York Freedom of Information Law (FOIL) on March 15, 2023. The request sought records related to water quality complaints and issues within the Albany, Schenectady, and Troy Metropolitan Statistical Area (MSA) from January 1, 2018, to the present. I have not received a response, acknowledgment, or any communication from the Niskayuna Consolidated Water District regarding my request. Pursuant to FOIL, the agency is required to respond within five business days.

As a reminder, my original request sought the following information:

1) The total number of water quality complaints received by your agency/authority/utility, broken down by year.
2) A detailed summary of each water quality complaint, including the date, location, nature of the complaint, any actions taken, and the resolution or current status.
3) Any reports, memos, or other documentation related to water quality complaints, investigations, and resolutions within the specified area and timeframe.

I followed up on my original request with two subsequent emails on April 11, 2023, and April 26, 2023, requesting a response or an update on the status of my request. However, I have not received any acknowledgment or response from the Niskayuna Consolidated Water District.

The summary denial of access to the requested records is in violation of FOIL. I respectfully request that you reconsider my request and provide the requested records or a valid explanation for withholding them within ten business days, as required by FOIL.

If my appeal is denied, please provide a written explanation for the denial and inform me of any further appeal procedures available under New York State law.

Thank you for your attention to this matter, and I look forward to receiving your response.

title: Clinton Library: WHORM files related to Zurab Tsereteli and "The Birth of a New World" Columbus statue

communications.subject: Freedom of Information Act Request: Clinton Library: WHORM files related to Zurab Tsereteli and "The Birth of a New World" Columbus statue
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Freedom of Information Act, I hereby request the following records:

White House Office of Records Management (WHORM) files related to the sculptor Zurab Tsereteli (Georgian: ზურაბ წერეთელი) and a sculpture of his of Christopher Columbus titled "The Birth of a New World."

For background: in 1997 the Moscow mayor's office offered a statue of Tsereteli's as a gift to the city of New York. Donald Trump lobbied to have the city allow him to place it at one of his developments. Mayor Giuliani lobbied against the statue, and for the next 18 years it was offered to other cities. 

Please search WHORM files for records related to Zurab Tsereteli and the late 1990s offer from the Russians for the statue. Keywords: Zurab Tsereteli, ზურაბ წერეთელი

For additional information: https://www.motherjones.com/politics/2016/06/trump-files-donald-almost-built-gigantic-christopher-columbus-statue/

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

title: County Assessor's Tax Roll Request (Manistee County)

communications.subject: Michigan Freedom of Information Act Request: County Assessor's Tax Roll Request (Manistee County)
communications.communication:
Dear Open Records FOIA Coordinator,

Pursuant to Michigan's Freedom of Information Act, MCL 15.231 et seq., I am requesting a copy of the Open Records county-wide assessment roll in its existing electronic format (CSV, DBF, or equivalent tabular export). If a single county-wide file does not exist, individual municipal-level exports that together cover all parcels in the county will satisfy this request.

Requested fields include, to the extent they exist in the record: parcel number, property address, owner name and mailing address, assessed value, state equalized value, taxable value, property classification, homestead/PRE status, school district, and any recorded sale date and sale price. I understand that many counties routinely provide similar datasets to commercial data aggregators such as Zillow and other real estate platforms, and I am requesting the same or comparable data.

I ask that this request be interpreted liberally, consistent with MCL 15.231(2). To the extent additional non-exempt fields exist in the record beyond those listed above, please include them. If the records are available for multiple years or assessment cycles, please include as broad a duration as is readily available rather than limiting the response to the current year alone. Where any question arises about whether to include or exclude non-exempt information, I respectfully ask that you err on the side of inclusion.

I am not requesting GIS data, parcel geometry, or any record that must be created — only a copy of existing electronic records. Please note that MCL 15.443(4) provides that the Enhanced Access to Public Records Act does not limit inspection and copying of public records under FOIA.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you.
Alexander Munro
Ypsilanti, MI
734-272-6612
awmunro@gmail.com

title: ALPR WA 2026: H: All ALPR Scan Records (Port of Seattle Police Department)

communications.subject: Washington Public Records Act Request: ALPR WA 2026: H: All ALPR Scan Records (Port of Seattle Police Department)
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

## PRESERVATION DEMAND ##

Pursuant to O'Neill v. City of Shoreline, 170 Wn.2d 138 (2010), and WAC 44-14-03005, your agency's obligation to preserve all records responsive to this request attaches upon receipt.

Please immediately:

1. Suspend any automated deletion, overwrite, or purge process that would destroy records falling within the scope of this request, whether operating on your agency's own infrastructure or on vendor-managed infrastructure over which your agency holds a contractual right of access or retrieval.

2. Issue a written instruction to your ALPR vendor(s) directing them to halt any automated deletion of records within the scope of this request pending its resolution.

## VENDOR-HELD RECORDS ##

In Rodriguez v. City of Stanwood & Sedro-Woolley (Skagit County Superior Court, No. 22-2-00477-29, Nov. 6, 2025), the court held that ALPR data held by a third-party SaaS vendor on behalf of a government agency is a public record subject to PRA disclosure, and that vendor custody does not relieve the agency of its production obligation. That ruling is currently on appeal to the Washington Court of Appeals, Division 1; the appeal does not stay the disclosure obligation.  Each request below that encompasses vendor-held records is made on this basis. Please retrieve and produce all responsive records held by your ALPR vendor(s) on your agency's behalf.

## ELECTRONIC REPRODUCTION AND THE DUTY TO PRODUCE ##

I seek existing records only. I understand that no agency is required to create a new record in response to a PRA request. However, producing a screenshot or other faithful reproduction of an existing electronic display is not creating a new record — it is reproducing an existing one, in the same manner as photocopying or scanning a paper document. Where a requested record exists only as a display within a software interface, a screenshot or faithful reproduction of that display, or an export of that display to any available file format (including CSV, PDF, or JSON), is an acceptable and required form of production.

## FEE REQUEST ##

I am requesting these records for research and public interest purposes, submitted through MuckRock, a nonprofit journalism platform. I request that your agency consider waiving any fees given that this request is submitted for research and public interest purposes and that electronic production of the requested records should entail minimal or no actual cost under RCW 42.56.120. For any anticipated costs, please notify me before incurring them so that I may prioritize or narrow the request. This notification requirement should not be construed as authorization to delay production of records for which no fee will be incurred pending resolution of fees for other records in this request

## RECORDS REQUESTED ##

Pursuant to the Washington Public Records Act, I hereby request the following records:

1. All existing ALPR scan records (e.g., stored in your agency's ALPR system or any system used to store, process, or query ALPR data, such as Axon Evidence.com, Flock Safety portal, or Motorola/Vigilant), including all metadata fields associated with each record as captured and stored by the system. This includes but is not limited to: license plate number, timestamp, GPS coordinates, camera or device identifier, lane identifier, direction of travel, confidence score, vehicle make, vehicle model, vehicle color, image file metadata, and any field capturing information about vehicle occupants or associated individuals.

2. All system audit logs associated with the records in item 1, as natively maintained in your agency's systems or your vendor's systems, reflecting every query performed against your agency's ALPR system, including for each query: the date and time, the user identifier or badge number of the querying user, the Originating Agency Identifier (ORI) of the querying agency, and the entry in the "reason for search," "justification," "purpose," or equivalent field. Where no justification was entered for a query, the log entry reflecting that absence is itself a responsive record and should be produced.

To facilitate partial rolling production while the full dataset is being compiled, please prioritize production of records associated with the following specific license plates. Prioritizing these plates does not limit or waive any part of this request:

	Seattle Police Department specialized and long-service fleet vehicles
	(from City of Seattle fleet records; plates may have been re-registered
	but vehicles are believed to remain in service):
	c. 50816D  — Surveillance trailer (Skywatch Sentinel, 2010)
	d. 50887D  — Hybrid command center (2007, est. replacement 2031)
	e. 54180D  — Police armored vehicle (2011, est. replacement 2031)
	f. 43500D  — Bomb squad trailer (2005)
	g. 26073D  — Bomb containment vessel trailer (1997)
	h. 41461D  — Communications van (2005)
	i. 44372D  — Command vehicle (2005)
	j. 54148D  — Riot fencing trailer (2011)
	k. 54362D  — CSI van with generator (2012)
	l. 50295D  — SWAT cutaway van (2008)
	m. 54117D  — CSI van (2011)
	n. 24208D  — Weapons vehicle (1987)
	
	Washington State fleet vehicles (by plate pattern):
	o. All plates with the prefix or suffix "WSP"
	p. All plates with the prefix "XMT"
	
	Other:
	a. BCH-478
	b. 826-ZVA

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 5 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Phil Mocek

title: FOIA Request – Traffic Studies (La Grange Rd / US-45) – Village of La Grange

communications.subject: Illinois Freedom of Information Act. Request: FOIA Request – Traffic Studies (La Grange Rd / US-45) – Village of La Grange
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Illinois Freedom of Information Act., I hereby request the following records:

Pursuant to the Illinois Freedom of Information Act (5 ILCS 140), I am requesting an electronic copy of the following public records maintained by the Illinois Department of Transportation (IDOT):

The three (3) most recent traffic studies (including but not limited to traffic volume counts, speed studies, or safety/crash analyses) conducted for La Grange Road (US-45) within the Village of La Grange (Cook County).

Request Details:

Format: I request these records be provided in an electronic format (PDF) via email.

Commercial Purpose: This request is not for a commercial purpose.

Fee Waiver: As a community advocate, I am requesting these records to better understand local public safety and infrastructure planning. I request a waiver of all fees. If there are fees, please contact me before proceeding.

Please provide a response within the statutory limit of five (5) business days. If any portion of this request is denied, please provide a written explanation for the denial and the name of the individual responsible for the decision.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 5 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Concerned Citizen of La Grange

communications.subject: RE: Illinois Freedom of Information Act. Request: FOIA Request – Traffic Studies (La Grange Rd / US-45) – Village of La Grange
communications.communication: Just following up on this request as it has been longer than 5 business day and I have no response.  Please let me know if there is a problem or anything that I can help with.

communications.subject: RE: Illinois Freedom of Information Act. Request: FOIA Request – Traffic Studies (La Grange Rd / US-45) – Village of La Grange
communications.communication:
Subject: SECOND FOLLOW-UP: FOIA Request – Traffic Studies (La Grange Rd / US-45) – [Original Submission Date]

To: IDOT FOIA Officer
From: Concerned Citizen of La Grange
Date: March 30, 2026

RE: NOTICE OF DEEMED DENIAL AND INTENT TO FILE REQUEST FOR REVIEW

To the IDOT FOIA Officer,

I am writing to submit a second follow-up regarding my FOIA request for the three most recent traffic studies for La Grange Road (US-45) within the Village of La Grange, originally submitted on [Insert Original Date, e.g., March 11, 2026].

As of today, March 30, 2026, I have received neither the requested records nor a statutory notification of an extension. My first follow-up on March 23, 2026, also remains unacknowledged.

Please be advised of the following:

Deemed Denial: Under 5 ILCS 140/3(d), IDOT’s failure to respond within the statutory timeframe constitutes a "deemed denial."

Waiver of Fees: Pursuant to 5 ILCS 140/3(d), because IDOT failed to respond within the time limits set forth in the Act, the agency has waived its right to charge any fees for the reproduction of these records.

Final Notice: This serves as my final attempt to resolve this matter directly with your office. If I do not receive the requested records or a status update by the close of business on Wednesday, April 1, 2026, I will immediately file a Request for Review with the Public Access Counselor (PAC) at the Illinois Attorney General’s Office to compel production.

I would prefer to resolve this without the intervention of the Attorney General. Please provide the electronic copies (PDF) of the traffic studies to this email address as originally requested.

Sincerely,
Concerned Citizen of La Grange

title: Sheriff use of force data (2017-2025) (Sheriff)

communications.subject: South Carolina Public Records Law Request: Sheriff use of force data (2017-2025) (Sheriff)
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the South Carolina Public Records Law, I hereby request the following records:

Use of force (UOF) data dating from January 1, 2017 to December 31, 2025. If possible, please provide this data in database or spreadsheet format and include the case number, incident date, incident time, subject injured indicator, subject injury force related info (e.g. Yes or No), subject age, subject gender, subject ethnicity, subject weapon type, incident area occurred, force type, officer division (e.g. law enforcement operations, detention/correctional operations, etc.), badge number and names associated with the officers involved. If one or more of these fields or dates is unavailable, provide all fields and/or information that is available responsive to my request. If no database or spreadsheet format is available, please provide copies of each individual use of force report or other report where use of force information is documented/reported by officers. Note, this is for all use of force incidents reported by your agency, not limited to only those incidents required to be reported under state or federal programs.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 15 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Samuel Sinyangwe

communications.subject: FW: South Carolina Public Records Law Request: Sheriff use of force data (2017-2025) (Sheriff)
communications.communication:
We have received your request. However, be advised due to your requesting information that is greater than 24 months old,  pursuant to S.C. Code Ann. Section 30-4-30 (2)(C ) I have 20 business days to inform you of receipt of your request, followed by an additional 35 calendar days to provide that information.


(C) Each public body, upon written request for records made under this chapter, shall within ten days (excepting Saturdays, Sundays, and legal public holidays) of the receipt of the request, notify the person making the request of its determination and the reasons for it; provided, however, that if the record is more than twenty-four months old at the date the request is made, the public body has twenty days (excepting Saturdays, Sundays, and legal public holidays) of the receipt to make this notification. This determination must constitute the final opinion of the public body as to the public availability of the requested public record, however, the determination is not required to include a final decision or express an opinion as to whether specific portions of the documents or information may be subject to redaction according to exemptions provided for by Section 30-4-40 or other state or federal laws. If the request is granted, the record must be furnished or made available for inspection or copying no later than thirty calendar days from the date on which the final determination was provided, unless the records are more than twenty-four months old, in which case the public body has no later than thirty-five calendar days from the date on which the final determination was provided. If a deposit as provided in subsection (B) is required by the public body, the record must be furnished or made available for inspection or copying no later than thirty calendar days from the date on which the deposit is received, unless the records are more than twenty-four months old, in which case the public body has no later than thirty-five calendar days from the date on which the deposit was received to fulfill the request. The full amount of the total cost must be paid at the time of the production of the request. If written notification of the determination of the public body as to the availability of the requested public record is neither mailed, electronically transmitted, nor personally delivered to the person requesting the document within the time set forth by this section, the request must be considered approved as to nonexempt records or information. Exemptions from disclosure as set forth in Section 30-4-40 or by other state or federal laws are not waived by the public body's failure to respond as set forth in this subsection. The various response, determination, and production deadlines provided by this subsection are subject to extension by written mutual agreement of the public body and the requesting party at issue, and this agreement shall not be unreasonably withheld.

[cid:image002.png@01DCA0C7.8B398150]

Hello,

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 10 calendar days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Joey Scott

title: Panattoni Email Metadata

communications.subject: The Missouri Sunshine Law Request: Panattoni Email Metadata
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the The Missouri Sunshine Law, I hereby request the following records:

Metadata for all email communications which included anyone with a Panattoni email (*@Panattoni.com) as either the sender and/or receiver, including being CCed or BCCed, between 01/01/2025 to the date this request is processed.

The specific metadata data points I'm interested in are: to address(es), from address, cc address(es), bcc address(es), sent date/time, received date/time, subject line, and names of all attachments. This request does not seek the contents of the emails themselves, and only the specified email metadata. Please do not include duplicate emails. Please provide me with the records in an Excel format.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 3 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Michael

communications.subject: Online Form Submission #24169 for Request for Public Information
communications.communication:
The City Clerk’s office is in receipt of your request.



Please note this request may take more than 72 hours to complete. You will be notified when it’s completed.

Be advised that the City of Joplin passed Resolution No. 2004-038 on the 16th day of August 2004 which pertains to charging a fee for obtaining information.  This resolution allows the City to charge for staff research time and for copying fees.



If requested, we will provide you with an estimate of charges before processing.  We do not waive fees for requests.


Holly Nagy

communications.communication:
Good afternoon,

A deposit of $38.72 is required to compile your request. If the cost exceeds $38.72 you will be required to make additional payment, if actual costs are less than the estimate, a refund will be processed.

IT Network Admin - 1 hour @ $38.72/hr = $38.72 – research/labor compiling responsive material.

Upon receipt of payment, we will begin processing your request.

Payment can be mailed to 602 S. Main Street, Joplin, MO 64801.  Please make payment out to City of Joplin and mark the envelope attn City Clerk.

Currently, we do not accept payments by phone or online. You may also drop off your payment at the Finance Department. Take your payment to the third floor and let them know you are paying for an open records request.   We are open Monday-Friday 8AM-5PM.


Holly Nagy

title: FOIA for who is the Thurston County Sheriff, Derek Sanders (Thurston County Auditor's Office)

communications.subject: Washington Public Records Act Request: FOIA for who is the Thurston County Sheriff, Derek Sanders (Thurston County Auditor's Office)
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Washington Public Records Act, I hereby request the following records:

This is a request under the Freedom of Information Act, The Sunshine Act 5 U.S.C. § 552b, and Washington Public Records Act Title 14 Chapter 44-14 and related. 

Please provide the records in electronic form, and produce them on a rolling basis as they become available for  Thurston County Sheriff, Derek Sanders.

If a record exists but cannot be produced, please state the reason and provide any non-exempt portions. If the record does not exist or is not held by your agency, please confirm which of these applies.

●A copy of their Public Official surety bond that is required by Washington Title 28A.330.060
● A copy of the blanket surety bond
● A copy of their Errors & Omissions (E&O), a Surety Liability Insurance policy, and the Duty of Care policy if applicable.
● A copy of their General Obligation Bonds if applicable.
● A copy of their general long-term bond if applicable.
● A copy of their Crime Policy if applicable.
● A copy of their Risk Management Policy if applicable.
● A copy of the following documents if applicable:
○ ACORD 125
○ ACORD 126
○ ACORD 127
○ ACORD 128
○ The documents should indicate the policy number and the insured amount of the policy.
● Certificate of Liability if applicable.
● Public Officials and/or any other bonds pertaining to proof of liability and policies.
Based on any and all losses of financial responsibility due to negligence or dishonesty.
Any and all based on the contract of terms and conditions.
● A copy of their faithful Performance Bond if applicable.
● A copy of their Fidelity Bond if applicable.
● A copy of their Public Employee Dishonesty Policy if applicable.
● A copy of their Public Employee Blanket Bond if applicable.
● A copy of their Statutory Bond if applicable.
● A copy of the power of attorney for the surety bond company.
● A copy of the Blanket Bond power of attorney for the surety bond company if applicable.
● A copy of their oath of office.
● A copy of their Anti Bribery and Corruption Statement.
● A copy of their Form NDS-1, Registration Statement Pursuant to the Foreign Agents Registration Act.

I am seeking information concerning their public official surety bond, or the Errors & Omissions (E&O), and or the Duty of Care policy that you are required by the state of Washington to obtain before swearing the oath of office.
If any document listed cannot be provided, please respond specifying which document(s) cannot be provided and the reason for each non-disclosure.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 5 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Anne Sackville

Anne Sackville
