communications.subject: [Records Center] Public Records Request :: P039039-121525
communications.communication:
--- Please respond above this line --- 

December 22, 2025 

Anne  Sackville 
MuckRock News, DEPT MR199500 263 Huntington Ave 
Boston, MA 02115 

Dear Anne Sackville: 

In accordance with the Public Records Act (PRA), RCW 42.56.520, this letter acknowledges receipt of your PRA request, submitted to and received by Thurston County on December 15, 2025. 

This request has been assigned tracking number P039039-121525. Please use this number in any communications concerning this request. 

Specifically, you requested: 
To Whom It May Concern: 

Pursuant to the Washington Public Records Act, I hereby request the following records: 

This is a request under the Freedom of Information Act, The Sunshine Act 5 U.S.C. § 552b, and Washington Public Records Act Title 14 Chapter 44-14 and related. 

Please provide the records in electronic form, and produce them on a rolling basis as they become available for Thurston County Sheriff, Derek Sanders. 

If a record exists but cannot be produced, please state the reason and provide any non-exempt portions. If the record does not exist or is not held by your agency, please confirm which of these applies. 

●A copy of their Public Official surety bond that is required by Washington Title 28A.330.060 
● A copy of the blanket surety bond 
● A copy of their Errors & Omissions (E&O), a Surety Liability Insurance policy, and the Duty of Care policy if applicable. 
● A copy of their General Obligation Bonds if applicable. 
● A copy of their general long-term bond if applicable. 
● A copy of their Crime Policy if applicable. 
● A copy of their Risk Management Policy if applicable. 
● A copy of the following documents if applicable: 
○ ACORD 125 
○ ACORD 126 
○ ACORD 127 
○ ACORD 128 
○ The documents should indicate the policy number and the insured amount of the policy. 
● Certificate of Liability if applicable. 
● Public Officials and/or any other bonds pertaining to proof of liability and policies. 
Based on any and all losses of financial responsibility due to negligence or dishonesty. 
Any and all based on the contract of terms and conditions. 
● A copy of their faithful Performance Bond if applicable. 
● A copy of their Fidelity Bond if applicable. 
● A copy of their Public Employee Dishonesty Policy if applicable. 
● A copy of their Public Employee Blanket Bond if applicable. 
● A copy of their Statutory Bond if applicable. 
● A copy of the power of attorney for the surety bond company. 
● A copy of the Blanket Bond power of attorney for the surety bond company if applicable. 
● A copy of their oath of office. 
● A copy of their Anti Bribery and Corruption Statement. 
● A copy of their Form NDS-1, Registration Statement Pursuant to the Foreign Agents Registration Act. 

I am seeking information concerning their public official surety bond, or the Errors & Omissions (E&O), and or the Duty of Care policy that you are required by the state of Washington to obtain before swearing the oath of office. 
If any document listed cannot be provided, please respond specifying which document(s) cannot be provided and the reason for each non-disclosure. 

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes. 

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not. 

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 5 business days, as the statute requires. 

Sincerely, 

Anne Sackville 

Upload documents directly: https://www.muckrock.com/respond/2150834/ 

We have begun a process to search for records responsive to your request. We reasonably believe that at least a first installment of public records (if any) responsive to this request will be available on or before April 03, 2026. 
Additionally, please note that some of the records you are seeking such as Oaths are considered court records and maintained within the official court file held by Thurston County Superior Court. Court records are provided to the public pursuant to GR 31, rather than the Public Records Act; therefore, you will want to submit a separate request (if you have not already done so) to the court directly. Information about how to request records from the court file is available on their website at this link: https://www.thurstoncountywa.gov/departments/county-clerk/obtaining-documents 

If public records become available in advance of this estimate, we will contact you promptly. Conversely, if the County encounters a need to extend our estimate, we will contact you with a revised estimated date. We will communicate with you and keep you informed of any updates regarding collecting and obtaining the responsive records as we move through the process. 

RCW 42.56.120 provides that the County may charge fees as authorized by law for responsive public records documents. 
If you have any questions you may contact me at 360-754-4998. 

Sincerely, 

Sara Meath 
Paralegal 
Prosecutor's Office

communications.subject: [Records Center] Public Records Request :: P039039-121525
communications.communication:
--- Please respond above this line --- 

April 02, 2026 

Anne  Sackville 
MuckRock News, DEPT MR199500 263 Huntington Ave 
Boston, MA 02115 

Dear Anne Sackville: 

This communication is regarding your public records request, #P039039-121525. 
The records responsive to your request are available for review and download via your account on the Public Records Center. 
If you experience any difficulty viewing the records, please disable any pop-up blockers on your internet browser, as these may block your access to the records. 
We have made no redactions nor withheld any records in response to this request. 
As we mentioned previously, you may wish to contact the Thurston County Superior Court Clerk's Office to check for any additional Bond records of interest they may maintain. Additionally, some records, such as Oaths, are considered recorded documents which are filed with the Thurston County Auditor's Office. These records do not fall within a public records request and are provided to the public through a different legal process than the Public Records Act, chapter 42.56 RCW. 

Information about how to request records from these departments may be found on the County's website at the links below: 
Thurston County Clerk's Office: https://www.thurstoncountywa.gov/departments/county-clerk/info/obtaining-copies-documents-filed-thurston-county-superior-court 
Recorded documents: https://www.thurstoncountywa.gov/departments/auditor/recording-services 
This is the final and definitive response to your records request, and your request is now closed. Please let us know at your earliest convenience if you believe we have misunderstood your records request or have omitted records that you believe your request covered. 
Please note that the PRA's one-year statute of limitations to seek judicial review has started to run because the County considers your request to have been fulfilled and does not intend to further respond to this request.  Should you wish to do so, you may also seek review pursuant to Thurston County Code 2.130.020. 

If you have any questions, you may contact me at 360-754-4998. 

Sincerely, 

Sara Meath 
Paralegal 
Prosecutor's Office

title: Maryland HHS response

communications.subject: Maryland Public Information Act Request: Maryland HHS response
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Maryland Public Information Act, I hereby request the following records:

Communications and correspondence with the U.S. Department of Health and Human Services regarding the Aug. 26, 2025, HHS letter requesting the Maryland Department of Health and Human Services remove content from the state's Personal Responsibility Education Program curricula and program materials. For reference, the letter can be found here: https://acf.gov/sites/default/files/documents/main/08.26.25-Combined-PREP-Letters.pdf.

I also request internal communications between any Maryland Department of Health and Human Services employees about the aforementioned letter. If the content changes were made, I request a copy of the material before it was changed as well as a copy of the material after it was changed.

I additionally request that, if possible, fees be waived as I believe this request is in the public interest. The requested documents will be made available to the general public free of charge as part of the public information service at MuckRock.com, processed by a representative of the news media/press and is made in the process of news gathering and not for commercial usage.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 10 calendar days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Ileana Garnand

communications.communication:

 Thank you for your email! 

Your request is governed by the Maryland Public Information Act (“MPIA”), Title 4 of the General Provisions Article of the Annotated Code of Maryland. 

The Maryland Department of Health (“MDH”) has received your records request on 01/12/26. It has been assigned tracking number 26-029. 

Please be advised that pursuant to the requirements of the Act, the MDH will respond to your request as soon as possible, which is expected to be within ten (10) business days, but not more than thirty (30) calendar days of your request. There may be instances where the MDH needs additional time to locate and/or review requested records. communications.communication:

Thank you for your January 21, 2026, revised request for publicly available
records related to the Maryland Department of Health (MDH) Personal
Responsibility Education Program, specifically:



   -

   “Communications and correspondence with the U.S. Department of Health
   and Human Services regarding the Aug. 26, 2025, HHS letter requesting the

   materials. For reference, the letter can be found here:
   https://acf.gov/sites/default/files/documents/main/08.26.25-Combined-PREP-Letters.pdf
   .


I also request internal communications between any Maryland Department of
Health and Human Services employees about the aforementioned letter. If the
content changes were made, I request a copy of the material before it was
changed as well as a copy of the material after it was changed.”

   Keywords include "PREP", "Personal Responsibility Education Program",
   "non-compliant", "Rights, Respect, Responsibility", "Making Proud Choices",
   "gender", "identity", "transgender", "Family and Youth Services Bureau",
   "FYSB", "Administration for Children and Families" and "ACF". Records do
   not need to include every keyword.

Reynolds, Hannah Bress, Iliana Siarmalis and Ben Wormser.

the status of your request.

Thank you,

title: Sheriff use of force data (2017-2025) under 50k population (Ulster County Sheriffs Office)

communications.subject: New York Freedom of Information Law Request: Sheriff use of force data (2017-2025) under 50k population (Ulster County Sheriffs Office)
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the New York Freedom of Information Law, I hereby request the following records:

Use of force (UOF) data dating from January 1, 2017 to December 31, 2025. If possible, please provide this data in database or spreadsheet format and include the case number, incident date, incident time, subject injured indicator, subject injury force related info (e.g. Yes or No), subject age, subject gender, subject ethnicity, subject weapon type, incident area occurred, force type, officer division (e.g. law enforcement operations, detention/correctional operations, etc.), badge number and names associated with the officers involved. If one or more of these fields is unavailable, provide all fields and/or information that is available responsive to my request. If no database or spreadsheet format is available, please provide copies of each individual use of force report or other report where use of force information is documented/reported by officers. Note, this is for all use of force incidents reported by your agency, not limited to only those incidents required to be reported under state or federal programs.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 5 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Samuel Sinyangwe

title: Freedom of Information Act Request – Plan Commission Reimbursable Charges

communications.subject: Illinois Freedom of Information Act. Request: Freedom of Information Act Request – Plan Commission Reimbursable Charges
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Illinois Freedom of Information Act., I hereby request the following records:

TO: Freedom of Information Officer

Village of La Grange

53 S. La Grange Road

La Grange, IL 60525

DATE: March 11, 2026

RE: Freedom of Information Act Request – Plan Commission Reimbursable Charges

To the Freedom of Information Officer:

This is a request for public records under the Illinois Freedom of Information Act (5 ILCS 140). I am requesting the following records regarding Plan Commission Cases 284, 285, 286, 287, 288, and 289:

Reimbursable Charge Records: All documents identifying or listing reimbursable charges, actual costs, or fees incurred by the Village in processing these specific applications.

Invoices: All complete invoices (including those from professional and technical consultants, legal counsel, court reporters, or secretarial services) associated with these cases.

Correspondence: All correspondence regarding the assessment, billing, or collection of these charges between Village staff, the applicants, the property owners, or third-party consultants.

Request for Fee Waiver (Public Interest)
Pursuant to 5 ILCS 140/6(c), I am requesting a waiver of all fees associated with this request. This request is in the public interest because:

Dissemination to the Public: The principal purpose is to access and disseminate information regarding the fiscal management and legal transparency of the Village’s Plan Commission proceedings to the general public.

Non-Commercial: This request is not for personal or commercial benefit.

Public Welfare: Understanding how public resources and reimbursable expenses are managed directly impacts the welfare and legal rights of the residents of La Grange.

Instructions for Response
Format: Please provide these records in an electronic format (PDF/Excel) to the email address provided below.

Statutory Deadlines: As a reminder, under 5 ILCS 140/3(d), if the Village fails to respond within the statutory timeframe, it forfeits the right to charge fees and may not treat this request as "unduly burdensome" under Section 3(g).

If any portion of this request is denied, please cite the specific exemption for each redaction and provide the name and title of the person responsible for the denial.

Thank you,
Concerned Citizen of La Grange

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 5 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Concerned Citizen of La Grange

communications.subject: RE: Illinois Freedom of Information Act. Request: Freedom of Information Act Request – Plan Commission Reimbursable Charges
communications.communication:
Subject: DEMAND FOR IMMEDIATE PRODUCTION: Notice of FOIA Violations (Request Submitted March 11, 2026)

To: FOIA Officer, Village of La Grange
From: Concerned Citizen of La Grange
Date: March 29, 2026

Dear FOIA Officer,

I am writing regarding the Freedom of Information Act (FOIA) request I submitted to the Village of La Grange on Wednesday, March 11, 2026, at 11:34 PM.

On Friday, March 27, 2026, the Village provided a partial production of records while withholding a large portion of the requested documents under the claim that fulfilling the request would be "unduly burdensome."

Please be advised that the Village’s use of the "unduly burdensome" exemption is legally invalid due to multiple procedural violations of the Illinois Freedom of Information Act (5 ILCS 140). The Village is now in a state of "deemed denial" and has forfeited its right to claim this exemption.

1. Untimely Extension and Forfeiture of Exemption (5 ILCS 140/3(d))
My request was received on Thursday, March 12. The statutory five-business-day deadline to respond or request an extension was the close of business (5:00 PM) on Thursday, March 19. The Village’s email attempting to invoke a five-day extension was not sent until 5:52 PM on March 19, well after business hours. Under 5 ILCS 140/3(d), a public body that fails to respond within the statutory timeframe "may not treat the request as unduly burdensome under subsection (g)." By missing the initial deadline, the Village legally forfeited the right to use this exemption.

2. Missed Extension Deadline
Even if the after-hours extension request had been valid, the new deadline for production would have been Thursday, March 26. The Village failed to produce any records until Friday, March 27, further cementing the request’s status as a deemed denial.

3. Failure to "Meet and Confer" (5 ILCS 140/3(g))
Furthermore, even if the Village had responded on time, it would have violated the mandatory procedural requirements of Section 3(g). The statute explicitly mandates that "before invoking this exemption, the public body shall extend to the person making the request an opportunity to confer with it in an attempt to reduce the request to manageable proportions." The Village issued a partial denial without ever offering this mandatory opportunity to confer.

Demand for Production
Because the Village failed to comply with the statutory deadlines set forth in 5 ILCS 140/3, the claim that the request is unduly burdensome is void. Furthermore, pursuant to Section 3(d), the Village has waived its right to charge any reproduction fees for this request.

I demand the immediate production of the remaining withheld records. If the Village does not provide the remaining documents by the close of business on Wednesday, April 1, 2026, I will file a formal Request for Review with the Public Access Counselor (PAC) at the Office of the Illinois Attorney General to compel production and report these procedural violations and/or explore filing a lawsuit with an attorney.

I look forward to your prompt compliance.

This request was to review the situation to consider a news article on the billing based on the concerns brought up in a public comment session, so understanding why one applicant was charged so differently from the others could be greatly in the public interest, pending the facts of the situation. Someone making the situation known in the public forum surely has pertinent communication with the village about the situation, and hopefully, an extensive explanation from the city staff.  

Sincerely,

Concerned Citizen of La Grange

title: Flock Safety search audits, network sharing, and general order

communications.subject: Washington Public Records Act Request: Flock Safety search audits, network sharing, and general order
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Washington Public Records Act, I hereby request the following records:

Records related to the department’s Flock Safety system:
1. Organization Audit in Flock. 
The report should include data logged from the period of September 1, 2025, to the date this request is processed. Per Flock's documentation, the Organization Audit is available within the Insights tab and is defined as searches done within the agency.

2. Network Audit in Flock.
The report should include data logged from the period of September 1, 2025, to the date this request is processed. Per Flock's documentation, the Network Audit is available within the Insights tab and is defined as searches of the organization’s Flock network by any agency in the Flock System.

3. A list of network share settings that include:
3a. Flock networks shared with me
3b. Networks that I’m sharing

Please provide records in their original electronic, machine readable format. For example, please provide csv or spreadsheet files where applicable.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 5 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Rose Terse

communications.subject: Your City of Lakewood public records request #25-1156 has been closed.
communications.communication:
City of Lakewood ************************************************************************ Record request #25-1156 has been closed. The closure reason supplied was: Thank you for your public records request. The final installment has been released for your review. We are closing the request by having released portions of all responsive records which are not exempt from disclosure by RCW 42.56 and/or other statutes, as detailed in the exemption log(s). Please note: The City's contractor, Flock Safety, provided the City with two versions of the requested network audit trails. The first one states "redacted" in certain fields. The second version has removed the word "redacted" and otherwise conforms to the City's process of redacting, albeit with asterisks, indicating where license plate numbers have been removed because they are exempt from disclosure. Please also be aware that the second version includes a longer time frame than the first. The City is prohibited from releasing Flock data, including Flock camera images, pursuant to RCW 42.56.050 (PRA - privacy); RCW 42.56.070 (1) (PRA - “other statute” exemptions); RCW 46.63.220 (11) (automated camera data is “not open to the public”); and the Federal Driver's Privacy Protection Act of 1993, 18 USCS (prohibits disclosure of personal information derived from an individual's motor vehicle record to anyone other than that individual, as personal information is defined in 18 USCS § 2725 (3).) Please see the Honorable Judge Kirkendoll's Order Granting Summary Judgment in Pierce County Cause No. 25-2-11627-7. However, the City may provide audit trails of Flock Safety cameras, with portions redacted to comply with the above legal requirements. Please see SB6002 which has now been signed into law and also prohibits data from being released. This request is now closed. The PRA’s one-year statute of limitations to seek judicial review has started to run because the agency does not intend to further address the request. While we consider this request to be concluded, the requester may ask follow-up questions within a reasonable time frame by contacting us via email at PRR@cityoflakewood.us or by phone at 253-983-7801 during business hours, Monday through Friday from 8:30 A.M. to 5:00 P.M. ************************************************************************ Questions about your request? Reply to this email or sign in to contact staff at City of Lakewood.

Technical support: See our help page

title: Retreat Expense Reports from BOARD of ED trustees

communications.subject: New York Freedom of Information Law Request: Retreat Expense Reports from BOARD of ED trustees
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the New York Freedom of Information Law, I hereby request the following records:

a) All expense reports and receipts related to retreats, conferences, and other events attended by the five trustees of East Williston School District Board of Education for the years 2022, 2023, 2024.
b) Any contracts, agreements, or invoices related to the organization and execution of these retreats, conferences, and events during the years 2022, 2023, 2024.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 5 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Mary Lee

communications.subject: RE: New York Freedom of Information Law Request #164667-00782640
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

I'm following up on the following New York Freedom of Information Law request, copied below, and originally submitted on June 5, 2024. You had previously indicated that it would be completed on Aug. 8, 2024. I wanted to check on the status of my request, and to see if there was a new estimated completion date.  You had assigned it reference number #164667-00782640.

Thanks for your help, and let me know if further clarification is needed.

MuckRock News
DEPT MR 164667
263 Huntington Ave
Boston, MA 02115
Attn: Mary Lee
	RE: 	FOIL Request dated June 5, 2024
Dear Ms. Lee:
	As you are aware, the East Williston Union Free School District (“District”) received your Freedom of Information Law (“FOIL”) request dated June 5, 2024.  Specifically, you requested the following: 
a)	All expense reports and receipts related to retreats, conferences, and other events attended by the five trustees of East Williston School District Board of Education for the years 2022, 2023, 2024.
b)	Any contracts, agreements, or invoices related to the organization and execution of these retreats, conferences, and events during the years 2022, 2023, 2024.
	Please be advised the District requires additional time to respond to and process your request.
	In determining a reasonable time for granting or denying a request, agencies, such as the District, shall “[1] consider the volume of a request, [2] the ease or difficulty in locating, retrieving or generating records, [3] the complexity of the request, [4] the need to review records to determine the extent to which they must be disclosed, [5] the number of requests received by the agency, [6] and similar factors that bear on an agency’s ability to grant access to records promptly and within a reasonable time.”  The following factors are applicable here: one, four, five, and six.  Specifically, your request seeks records that must be reviewed to determine whether an exception to FOIL exists and requires substantial effort to prepare (i.e., copy, scan and redact).  This is in addition to the other tasks the District must undertake. 
As such, it is anticipated that the District will either grant or deny your request on or before September 6, 2024. 
Pursuant to FOIL, if it is determined that your request is denied in-whole or in-part, you have the right to appeal a denial of any portion of your request within thirty (30) days to the Superintendent of Schools, Dr. Danielle Gately, East Williston Union Free School District, 11 Bacon Road, Old Westbury, New York 11568. You will be advised in writing of the Superintendent’s decision of your appeal within ten (10) business days following receipt of any written appeal. Pub. Off. Law § 89(4)(a).
									Sincerely, 
								
									DIANE CASTONGUAY
									Records Access Officer

communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

I'm following up on the following New York Freedom of Information Law request, copied below, and originally submitted on June 5, 2024. You had previously indicated that it would be completed on Dec. 4, 2024. I wanted to check on the status of my request, and to see if there was a new estimated completion date.

Thanks for your help, and let me know if further clarification is needed.

December 4, 2024

MuckRock News
DEPT MR 164667
263 Huntington Ave
Boston, MA 02115
Attn: Mary Lee
	RE: 	FOIL Request dated June 5, 2024
Dear Ms. Lee:
	As you are aware, the East Williston Union Free School District (“District”) received your Freedom of Information Law (“FOIL”) request dated June 5, 2024.  Specifically, you requested the following: 
a)	All expense reports and receipts related to retreats, conferences, and other events attended by the five trustees of East Williston School District Board of Education for the years 2022, 2023, 2024.
b)	Any contracts, agreements, or invoices related to the organization and execution of these retreats, conferences, and events during the years 2022, 2023, 2024.
	Please be advised the District requires additional time to respond to and process your request.
	In determining a reasonable time for granting or denying a request, agencies, such as the District, shall “[1] consider the volume of a request, [2] the ease or difficulty in locating, retrieving or generating records, [3] the complexity of the request, [4] the need to review records to determine the extent to which they must be disclosed, [5] the number of requests received by the agency, [6] and similar factors that bear on an agency’s ability to grant access to records promptly and within a reasonable time.”  The following factors are applicable here: one, four, five, and six.  Specifically, your request seeks records that must be reviewed to determine whether an exception to FOIL exists and requires substantial effort to prepare (i.e., copy, scan and redact).  This is in addition to the other tasks the District must undertake. 
As such, it is anticipated that the District will either grant or deny your request on or before January 6, 2025. 
Pursuant to FOIL, if it is determined that your request is denied in-whole or in-part, you have the right to appeal a denial of any portion of your request within thirty (30) days to the Superintendent of Schools, Dr. Danielle Gately, East Williston Union Free School District, 11 Bacon Road, Old Westbury, New York 11568. You will be advised in writing of the Superintendent’s decision of your appeal within ten (10) business days following receipt of any written appeal. Pub. Off. Law § 89(4)(a).
									Sincerely, 
								
									DIANE CASTONGUAY
									Records Access Officer

communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

I'm following up on the following New York Freedom of Information Law request, copied below, and originally submitted on June 5, 2024. You had previously indicated that it would be completed on Feb. 4, 2025. I wanted to check on the status of my request, and to see if there was a new estimated completion date.

Thanks for your help, and let me know if further clarification is needed.

February 4, 2025
RE: 164667-00782640

MuckRock News
DEPT MR 164667
263 Huntington Ave
Boston, MA 02115

Attn: Mary Lee

	RE: 	Partial Determination to FOIL Request dated June 5, 2024

Dear Ms. Lee:

This letter serves as a partial determination concerning your email request dated June 5, 2024 for records pursuant to the Freedom of Information Law (“FOIL”).  Specifically, you requested the following: 
a)	All expense reports and receipts related to retreats, conferences, and other events attended by the five trustees of East Williston School District Board of Education for the years 2022, 2023, 2024.
b)	Any contracts, agreements, or invoices related to the organization and execution of these retreats, conferences, and events during the years 2022, 2023, 2024.
Response to Request “a”:
Enclosed please find a partial production of records relating to this request.  Due to the volume of this request and the need to review such records to determine whether an exception to FOIL exists, please be advised the District requires additional time to process and complete its response to this request, as set forth below.     
With respect to the partial production, your request is granted in part and denied in part, insofar as the requested records are being provided to you with redactions, as explained below:
•	Public Officers Law §87(2)(i) permits an agency to deny access to records or portions thereof that, “if disclosed, would jeopardize the capacity of an agency or an entity that has shared information with an agency to guarantee the security of its information technology assets, such assets encompassing both electronic information systems and infrastructures.” Disclosure of information such as bank account numbers and check numbers could enable a person to gain access to or alter or adversely affect electronic information or electronic information systems, as such, they may justifiably be withheld.  § 87(2)(i). See FOIL-AO-14921 (2004); FOIL-AO-13948 (2003); FOIL-AO-13143 (2002); FOIL-AO-F9325 (1996).
•	Portions of the requested records which, if disclosed, would constitute an unwarranted invasion of personal privacy have been withheld and/or identifying details redacted pursuant to Public Officers Law §§ 87(2)(b), 89(2).  
Accordingly, the enclosed records have been redacted in accordance with the above. 
As stated above, the District requires additional time to respond to and process your request.  In determining a reasonable time for granting or denying a request, agencies, such as the District, shall “[1] consider the volume of a request, [2] the ease or difficulty in locating, retrieving or generating records, [3] the complexity of the request, [4] the need to review records to determine the extent to which they must be disclosed, [5] the number of requests received by the agency, [6] and similar factors that bear on an agency’s ability to grant access to records promptly and within a reasonable time.”  The following factors are applicable here: one, four, five, and six.  Specifically, your request seeks records that must be reviewed to determine whether an exception to FOIL exists and requires substantial effort to prepare (i.e., copy, scan and redact).  This is in addition to the other tasks the District must undertake. 
As to the remainder of your request, it is anticipated the District will either grant or deny, in whole or in part, your request on or before March 5, 2025.  

Response to Request “b”:
Enclosed please find a partial production of records relating to this request.  Due to the volume of this request and the need to review such records to determine whether an exception to FOIL exists, please be advised the District requires additional time to process and complete its response to this request, as set forth below.     
With respect to the partial production, your request is granted in part and denied in part, insofar as the requested records are being provided to you with redactions, as explained below:
•	Public Officers Law §87(2)(i) permits an agency to deny access to records or portions thereof that, “if disclosed, would jeopardize the capacity of an agency or an entity that has shared information with an agency to guarantee the security of its information technology assets, such assets encompassing both electronic information systems and infrastructures.”  
Accordingly, the enclosed records have been redacted in accordance with the above. 
As stated above, the District requires additional time to respond to and process your request.  In determining a reasonable time for granting or denying a request, agencies, such as the District, shall “[1] consider the volume of a request, [2] the ease or difficulty in locating, retrieving or generating records, [3] the complexity of the request, [4] the need to review records to determine the extent to which they must be disclosed, [5] the number of requests received by the agency, [6] and similar factors that bear on an agency’s ability to grant access to records promptly and within a reasonable time.”  The following factors are applicable here: one, four, five, and six.  Specifically, your request seeks records that must be reviewed to determine whether an exception to FOIL exists and requires substantial effort to prepare (i.e., copy, scan and redact).  This is in addition to the other tasks the District must undertake. 
As to the remainder of your request, it is anticipated the District will either grant or deny, in whole or in part, your request on or before March 5, 2025.  
Pursuant to FOIL, if it is determined that your request is denied in whole or in part, you have the right to appeal a denial of any portion of your request within thirty (30) days to the Superintendent of Schools, Dr. Danielle Gately, East Williston Union Free School District, 11 Bacon Road, Old Westbury, New York 11568. You will be advised in writing of the Superintendent’s decision of your appeal within ten (10) business days following receipt of any written appeal. Pub. Off. Law § 89(4)(a).
					
			
Sincerely, 

DIANE CASTONGUAY
Records Access Officer
Enclosures

communications.subject: FOIL request dated June 5, 2024
communications.communication:
*VIA EMAIL:* *164667-00782640@requests.muckrock.com*
<164667-00782640@requests.muckrock.com>



MuckRock News
DEPT MR 164667
263 Huntington Ave
Boston, MA  02115

Attn: Mary Lee



            *RE:     FOIL Request dated June 5, 2024*



Dear Ms. Lee:

As you know, on February 4, 2025 and March 5, 2025, the District provided
you with a partial determination concerning your email request dated June
5, 2024 for records pursuant to the Freedom of Information Law
(“FOIL”).  Specifically,
you requested the following:

a)      All expense reports and receipts related to retreats, conferences,
and other events attended by the five trustees of East Williston School
District Board of Education for the years 2022, 2023, 2024.

b)      Any contracts, agreements, or invoices related to the organization
and execution of these retreats, conferences, and events during the years
2022, 2023, 2024.

On June 27, 2025, July 16, 2025, August 13, 2025, September 11, 2025, and
October 9, 2025, the District provided a letter making a good faith effort
to discern the parameters of your document requests and requested
clarification of your requests.  The District has not received a response
with respect to its requests for clarification.  As such, we have provided
below our prior requests for clarification.



The District is making a good faith effort to discern the parameters of
your document requests and is requesting clarification of your requests
with respect to the years for expense reports, receipts, contracts,
agreements or invoices referred to in requests “a” and “b” above.
Specifically, the District maintains its records on a school year basis
from July 1st through June 30th.  Your request indicates specific years but
does not specify whether you are referring to the calendar year or the
school year. We request clarification as to the time parameters of your
requests.



Please advise the undersigned if you intend to clarify your request.  To
provide you with additional time to clarify, we are extending the timeline
for the District’s response to December 9, 2025.







Pursuant to FOIL, if it is determined that your request is denied in whole
or in part, you have the right to appeal a denial of any portion of your
request within thirty (30) days to the Superintendent of Schools, Dr.
Danielle Gately, East Williston Union Free School District, 11 Bacon Road,
Old Westbury, New York 11568. You will be advised in writing of the
Superintendent’s decision of your appeal within ten (10) business days
following receipt of any written appeal. Pub. Off. Law § 89(4)(a).



Regards,

Diane Castonguay, C.P.A.
Deputy Superintendent
East Williston School District

title: FOIA Request – Jeffrey Epstein Records

communications.subject: Freedom of Information Act Request: FOIA Request – Jeffrey Epstein Records
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Freedom of Information Act, I hereby request the following records:

that reasonably describe investigative files, communications, reports, interviews, and internal memoranda pertaining to:

Jeffrey Edward Epstein
DOB: January 20, 1953
DOD: August 10, 2019

This request includes but is not limited to:

Any and all criminal investigations or case files involving Epstein

Internal memos, emails, or communications referencing Epstein

Records of surveillance, interviews, or seized materials

Any documents pertaining to associated cases or persons, where Epstein is referenced

Please include records from the earliest available date through the present. Given Mr. Epstein’s death and the extensive public interest in this case, I believe no FOIA exemptions (including 7(C)) would justify withholding most materials.

I request that all records be delivered in digital format (PDF preferred), via email attachment. This request is made for public interest purposes and not for commercial use. If fees exceed $25, please notify me in advance.

Thank you for your time and compliance. I look forward to your response within the 20 business days required under the statute.

Sincerely,
Kaycie Crossley

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 20 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Kaycie Crossley

title: Request to Department of Defense for all 2025 film and TV collaborations

communications.subject: Freedom of Information Act Request: Request to Department of Defense for all 2025 film and TV collaborations
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Freedom of Information Act, I hereby request the following records:

A list of all the films and television productions that the U.S. Department of War has supported, in any capacity whatsoever, during the year 2025. Such a list, full or partial, maybe include, but should not limited to, entertainment liaison (ELO) reports or similar reports. I request that you search for these documents in any location where they might be located, including but not limited to the entertainment liaison offices and communications HQ.

I am the Director of Research Operations at a 501(c)(3) non-profit organization called The Decoding Culture Foundation as well as the editor-in-chief and a contributor to a research magazine published by said foundation titled "Cultural Engineering Studies." These documents will be used for public education and research purposes. I therefore request a waiver of all fees that may otherwise apply. Attached is a document with organization letterhead confirming my position, as well as the organization's 501(c)(3) application and approval form.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 20 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Brett Carollo

title: ALPR WA 2026: H: All ALPR Scan Records (Auburn Police Department)

communications.subject: Washington Public Records Act Request: ALPR WA 2026: H: All ALPR Scan Records (Auburn Police Department)
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

## PRESERVATION DEMAND ##

Pursuant to O'Neill v. City of Shoreline, 170 Wn.2d 138 (2010), and WAC 44-14-03005, your agency's obligation to preserve all records responsive to this request attaches upon receipt.

Please immediately:

1. Suspend any automated deletion, overwrite, or purge process that would destroy records falling within the scope of this request, whether operating on your agency's own infrastructure or on vendor-managed infrastructure over which your agency holds a contractual right of access or retrieval.

2. Issue a written instruction to your ALPR vendor(s) directing them to halt any automated deletion of records within the scope of this request pending its resolution.

## VENDOR-HELD RECORDS ##

In Rodriguez v. City of Stanwood & Sedro-Woolley (Skagit County Superior Court, No. 22-2-00477-29, Nov. 6, 2025), the court held that ALPR data held by a third-party SaaS vendor on behalf of a government agency is a public record subject to PRA disclosure, and that vendor custody does not relieve the agency of its production obligation. That ruling is currently on appeal to the Washington Court of Appeals, Division 1; the appeal does not stay the disclosure obligation.  Each request below that encompasses vendor-held records is made on this basis. Please retrieve and produce all responsive records held by your ALPR vendor(s) on your agency's behalf.

## ELECTRONIC REPRODUCTION AND THE DUTY TO PRODUCE ##

I seek existing records only. I understand that no agency is required to create a new record in response to a PRA request. However, producing a screenshot or other faithful reproduction of an existing electronic display is not creating a new record — it is reproducing an existing one, in the same manner as photocopying or scanning a paper document. Where a requested record exists only as a display within a software interface, a screenshot or faithful reproduction of that display, or an export of that display to any available file format (including CSV, PDF, or JSON), is an acceptable and required form of production.

## FEE REQUEST ##

I am requesting these records for research and public interest purposes, submitted through MuckRock, a nonprofit journalism platform. I request that your agency consider waiving any fees given that this request is submitted for research and public interest purposes and that electronic production of the requested records should entail minimal or no actual cost under RCW 42.56.120. For any anticipated costs, please notify me before incurring them so that I may prioritize or narrow the request. This notification requirement should not be construed as authorization to delay production of records for which no fee will be incurred pending resolution of fees for other records in this request

## RECORDS REQUESTED ##

Pursuant to the Washington Public Records Act, I hereby request the following records:

1. All existing ALPR scan records (e.g., stored in your agency's ALPR system or any system used to store, process, or query ALPR data, such as Axon Evidence.com, Flock Safety portal, or Motorola/Vigilant), including all metadata fields associated with each record as captured and stored by the system. This includes but is not limited to: license plate number, timestamp, GPS coordinates, camera or device identifier, lane identifier, direction of travel, confidence score, vehicle make, vehicle model, vehicle color, image file metadata, and any field capturing information about vehicle occupants or associated individuals.

2. All system audit logs associated with the records in item 1, as natively maintained in your agency's systems or your vendor's systems, reflecting every query performed against your agency's ALPR system, including for each query: the date and time, the user identifier or badge number of the querying user, the Originating Agency Identifier (ORI) of the querying agency, and the entry in the "reason for search," "justification," "purpose," or equivalent field. Where no justification was entered for a query, the log entry reflecting that absence is itself a responsive record and should be produced.

To facilitate partial rolling production while the full dataset is being compiled, please prioritize production of records associated with the following specific license plates. Prioritizing these plates does not limit or waive any part of this request:

	Seattle Police Department specialized and long-service fleet vehicles
	(from City of Seattle fleet records; plates may have been re-registered
	but vehicles are believed to remain in service):
	c. 50816D  — Surveillance trailer (Skywatch Sentinel, 2010)
	d. 50887D  — Hybrid command center (2007, est. replacement 2031)
	e. 54180D  — Police armored vehicle (2011, est. replacement 2031)
	f. 43500D  — Bomb squad trailer (2005)
	g. 26073D  — Bomb containment vessel trailer (1997)
	h. 41461D  — Communications van (2005)
	i. 44372D  — Command vehicle (2005)
	j. 54148D  — Riot fencing trailer (2011)
	k. 54362D  — CSI van with generator (2012)
	l. 50295D  — SWAT cutaway van (2008)
	m. 54117D  — CSI van (2011)
	n. 24208D  — Weapons vehicle (1987)
	
	Washington State fleet vehicles (by plate pattern):
	o. All plates with the prefix or suffix "WSP"
	p. All plates with the prefix "XMT"
	
	Other:
	a. BCH-478
	b. 826-ZVA

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 5 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Phil Mocek

communications.subject: Public Records Request :: P049079-031226
communications.communication:
RE: PUBLIC RECORDS REQUEST of 3/12/2026, Reference # P049079-031226 
 
Dear Phil Mocek, 
 
 
The City of Auburn received your public records request on 3/12/2026, regarding “March 12, 2026 
To Whom It May Concern: 
## PRESERVATION DEMAND ## 
Pursuant to O'Neill v. City of Shoreline, 170 Wn.2d 138 (2010), and WAC 44-14-03005, your agency's obligation to preserve all records responsive to this request attaches upon receipt. 
Please immediately: 
1. Suspend any automated deletion, overwrite, or purge process that would destroy records falling within the scope of this request, whether operating on your agency's own infrastructure or on vendor-managed infrastructure over which your agency holds a contractual right of access or retrieval. 
2. Issue a written instruction to your ALPR vendor(s) directing them to halt any automated deletion of records within the scope of this request pending its resolution. 
## VENDOR-HELD RECORDS ## 
In Rodriguez v. City of Stanwood & Sedro-Woolley (Skagit County Superior Court, No. 22-2-00477-29, Nov. 6, 2025), the court held that ALPR data held by a third-party SaaS vendor on behalf of a government agency is a public record subject to PRA disclosure, and that vendor custody does not relieve the agency of its production obligation. That ruling is currently on appeal to the Washington Court of Appeals, Division 1; the appeal does not stay the disclosure obligation. Each request below that encompasses vendor-held records is made on this basis. Please retrieve and produce all responsive records held by your ALPR vendor(s) on your agency's behalf. 
## ELECTRONIC REPRODUCTION AND THE DUTY TO PRODUCE ## 
I seek existing records only. I understand that no agency is required to create a new record in response to a PRA request. However, producing a screenshot or other faithful reproduction of an existing electronic display is not creating a new record — it is reproducing an existing one, in the same manner as photocopying or scanning a paper document. Where a requested record exists only as a display within a software interface, a screenshot or faithful reproduction of that display, or an export of that display to any available file format (including CSV, PDF, or JSON), is an acceptable and required form of production. 
## FEE REQUEST ## 
I am requesting these records for research and public interest purposes, submitted through MuckRock, a nonprofit journalism platform. I request that your agency consider waiving any fees given that this request is submitted for research and public interest purposes and that electronic production of the requested records should entail minimal or no actual cost under RCW 42.56.120. For any anticipated costs, please notify me before incurring them so that I may prioritize or narrow the request. This notification requirement should not be construed as authorization to delay production of records for which no fee will be incurred pending resolution of fees for other records in this request 
## RECORDS REQUESTED ## 
Pursuant to the Washington Public Records Act, I hereby request the following records: 
1. All existing ALPR scan records (e.g., stored in your agency's ALPR system or any system used to store, process, or query ALPR data, such as Axon Evidence.com, Flock Safety portal, or Motorola/Vigilant), including all metadata fields associated with each record as captured and stored by the system. This includes but is not limited to: license plate number, timestamp, GPS coordinates, camera or device identifier, lane identifier, direction of travel, confidence score, vehicle make, vehicle model, vehicle color, image file metadata, and any field capturing information about vehicle occupants or associated individuals. 
2. All system audit logs associated with the records in item 1, as natively maintained in your agency's systems or your vendor's systems, reflecting every query performed against your agency's ALPR system, including for each query: the date and time, the user identifier or badge number of the querying user, the Originating Agency Identifier (ORI) of the querying agency, and the entry in the "reason for search," "justification," "purpose," or equivalent field. Where no justification was entered for a query, the log entry reflecting that absence is itself a responsive record and should be produced. 
To facilitate partial rolling production while the full dataset is being compiled, please prioritize production of records associated with the following specific license plates. Prioritizing these plates does not limit or waive any part of this request: 
Seattle Police Department specialized and long-service fleet vehicles 
(from City of Seattle fleet records; plates may have been re-registered 
but vehicles are believed to remain in service): 
c. 50816D — Surveillance trailer (Skywatch Sentinel, 2010) 
d. 50887D — Hybrid command center (2007, est. replacement 2031) 
e. 54180D — Police armored vehicle (2011, est. replacement 2031) 
f. 43500D — Bomb squad trailer (2005) 
g. 26073D — Bomb containment vessel trailer (1997) 
h. 41461D — Communications van (2005) 
i. 44372D — Command vehicle (2005) 
j. 54148D — Riot fencing trailer (2011) 
k. 54362D — CSI van with generator (2012) 
l. 50295D — SWAT cutaway van (2008) 
m. 54117D — CSI van (2011) 
n. 24208D — Weapons vehicle (1987) 

Washington State fleet vehicles (by plate pattern): 
o. All plates with the prefix or suffix "WSP" 
p. All plates with the prefix "XMT" 

Other: 
a. BCH-478 
b. 826-ZVA” 
Your request will be forwarded to the applicable department(s) for response. 
You will be notified regarding the status of your request in 5 business days per RCW 42.56.520. 
 
Thank you for your submission, 
City of Auburn 
City Clerk’s Office

RE: PUBLIC RECORDS REQUEST of March 12, 2026, Reference # P049079-031226 Dear Phil: Pursuant to RCW 42.56.520 and Soter v. Cowles Pub. Co, this letter acknowledges that the City has received your above referenced public records request on 3/12/2026. Due to the number of requests the City is currently processing, and the time period needed to gather and review responsive records for potential exemptions, redactions and third party notices, the City estimates that it will take up to approximately 30 calendar -days from the date of this letter to respond to your request (either in full, or partially through a first installment of records). Please note the following helpful information as the City processes your records request: 
 
  *  The City responds to its numerous Public Records Requests in the order that they are received 
  *  The City may respond to larger records requests by providing responsive records in installments 
  *  The City's production of records to you may require you to pay fees and costs as allowed by law If your request seeks police-related records: these requests frequently generate large sets of responsive records. The City's practice is to respond by providing requested records in installments. First installments typically include responsive documents such as police reports, CAD incident reports, and photos. Later installments may include police in-car video or body camera footage. You will be provided a time estimate for the next installment as each installment becomes available. Narrowing the scope of your request may result in fewer installments, and therefore reduced overall response time. If you are interested in narrowing the scope of your request in any way, please respond to this message or contact our office at (253) 931-3039. If the City needs additional information to clarify your request, or to modify its estimated response date above, City staff will contact you. communications.subject: Public Records Request :: P049079-031226
communications.communication:
--- Please respond above this line --- 
Thank you for your recent public records request submitted to the City. This message is to provide an update regarding the portion of your request that seeks records that are now governed by SB 6002, also known as the Driver Privacy Act, which was signed into law on March 30, 2026, under Chapter 239, 2026 Laws. This new law became effective on the date Governor Ferguson signed it. SB 6002 establishes new statewide privacy protections for data generated by Automated License Plate Readers (ALPRs). Under this law, ALPR data is expressly exempt from disclosure under the Public Records Act. This includes license plate scans, images, timestamps, location information, and any metadata or information derived from ALPR detections. Because the Legislature designated this information as confidential, the City is prohibited from releasing ALPR data in response to public records requests. As a result, no ALPR records responsive to your request can be provided. If your request also includes other types of records—such as policies, contracts, correspondence, or administrative documents—those items are not covered by the exemption and will continue to be processed separately. We will follow up with you regarding any remaining responsive records. Shawn Campbell City Clerk 
To monitor the progress or update this request please log into the Public Records Center. (https://u8387778.ct.sendgrid.net/ls/click?upn=u001.VEsWrlMiJDg0mXafq1mEXf-2FJHL088NkuuvU1iB-2FA4tGwkjapw4gv521Cmc17zM-2FAMuTD7aNupvvylTsW9w9-2B35MwZrabQ7F0moBUcQpp-2B2PkAClbgJAGcZb-2BmgsGj8RlDs03_6334xWMMKo-2BpZch8yxsU-2FtOVmpIgoHGODIqD6CJheSXs1GdTutcELJzlqpbCSH-2BVztEPzshpmWWYxOchXziKazlquotSm7CtbC5UrnwTsak-2Fz3J-2FwfAorPSdPVO0zPPw568Ff4UiOTN00nKaGFqKm3CTXtHN3AvYtxCWx80iuFmdZn37Vff1-2BPBg-2FyvKIM-2BIc1mrdPug9kXE4mOYrOQL-2F6ODr3cvqp51ArRao0gOgttFcaj2uUPo-2F1cMWfm44KX4A6IkIrHZJfFyUlumMnivrbUAHUnxQU6Zk7FZEXWcR4dnv9YBlW9AoUxgCcv-2B-2BNoAV1bBk5Y-2BW8mqtUqR9c1Ay4F8CH6YYeJLzHc85483qcJpvmaH-2BrnBsYC19cjo4hqL)

title: Contract and documents related to SmartPass

communications.subject: Illinois Freedom of Information Act. Request: Contract and documents related to SmartPass
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Illinois Freedom of Information Act., I hereby request the following records:

"The Jayhawk Weekly" newsletter dated September 7, 2025 describes the SmartPass System that was adopted by District 97 middle schools. I am seeking materials related to the district's use of SmartPass. Specifically, I am requesting the following:

1.  All contracts, licenses, Memorandums of Understanding, data agreements, services agreement, letters of intent, State of Work documents, Scope of Work documents, and other agreement documents for the SmartPass system that were active any time between 1/1/2023 and the day this request is fulfilled.
    
2.  All usage policies, data retention policies, instructions to staff, and other materials that describe the District’s implementation and use of SmartPass.
    
3.  User guide or similar for the SmartPass system.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

title: West Virginia DOC Prison-Month Level

communications.subject: West Virginia Freedom of Information Act Request: West Virginia DOC Prison-Month Level
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the West Virginia Freedom of Information Act, I hereby request the following records:

This is a request under the FOIA Records Request. I would like to request both that a document containing facility-month level data from January 2024 through December 2025 be provided on the below measures for every facility managed or owned by West Virginia Division of Corrections and Rehabilitation:

* Overall staff budgeted positions (per prison, per month)
* Overall staff filled positions (per prison, per month)
* Custody staff budgeted positions (per prison, per month)
* Custody staff filled positions (per prison, per month)
* Average hours of overall staff overtime (per prison, per month)
* Average hours of custody staff overtime (per prison, per month)
* Average hours of overall staff sick leave (per prison, per month)
* Average hours of custody staff sick leave (per prison, per month)
* Overall population count (per prison, per month)
* Total number of infractions (per prison, per month)
* Total number of violent infractions (including the department’s definition of which infractions are defined as violent) (per prison, per month)
* Total number in restricted housing (per prison, per month)
* Total number of appeals from incarcerated individuals (per prison, per month)

I am also requesting the name and email of the current, acting, and/or incoming wardens for each facility managed or owned by West Virginia Division of Corrections and Rehabilitation. In order to help to determine my status to assess fees: I am affiliated with an educational institution, and this request is made for a scholarly or scientific purpose and not for commercial use.

Thank you for your consideration of this request.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 5 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Jessie Harney

title: Lake Geneva Wisconsin Canidates Forum Altercation

communications.subject: Wisconsin Open Records Act Request: Lake Geneva Wisconsin Canidates Forum Altercation
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Wisconsin Open Records Act, I hereby request the following records:

RE: Wisconsin Open Records Request — Ames/Hoiland Altercation, March 18, 2026 — Candidates Debate

Dear Chief 

Pursuant to Wisconsin Statute § 19.35(1)(a) and the Wisconsin Public Records Law, I hereby request access to and copies of all records in the possession, custody, or control of the Lake Geneva Police Department relating to an altercation that occurred on March 18, 2026, between Cheri Ames and Joel Hoiland at or in connection with the Lake Geneva aldermanic candidates debate held that evening.

RECORDS REQUESTED

I request all of the following records without limitation as to form or format:

1. Incident and Police Reports
All initial incident reports, supplemental reports, narratives, and the final investigative report and conclusions prepared by any officer involved in responding to or investigating the March 18, 2026 altercation.

2. Video and Audio Recordings
All video recordings, body-worn camera footage, dashcam recordings, surveillance video, and any other audio or visual recordings captured by Lake Geneva Police Department personnel in connection with this incident, including recordings made at or near the debate venue.

3. Witness and Party Statements
All written, recorded, or transcribed statements obtained from any party, witness, or individual in connection with the investigation of this altercation, including but not limited to statements from Cheri Ames, Joel Hoiland, and civilian witness Joe Esposito.

4. Joe Esposito Witness Statement and Related Records
All records relating to the statement provided by civilian witness Joe Esposito, including his written or recorded statement, any follow-up communications between Mr. Esposito and the Department, and any records reflecting how his account was considered in the investigation.

5. Communications
All internal communications, emails, text messages, radio logs, CAD (Computer Aided Dispatch) records, and dispatch audio relating to the incident, including the initial call for service, officer response, and any subsequent departmental communications regarding the matter.

6. Investigative Notes and Materials
All field notes, investigative memoranda, photographs, and any other documents created or collected in the course of investigating this incident.

7. Final Report and Conclusions
The final police department report, findings, and conclusions resulting from the investigation of this altercation, including any supervisory review or approval of those conclusions.

8. Disposition Records
Any records reflecting the outcome of the investigation, including any referrals to the Walworth County District Attorney's office, charging decisions, declination of prosecution, or other final disposition of the matter.

FORMAT AND DELIVERY

I request that records be provided in electronic format (PDF or native digital format) via email. If any records exist only in physical form, I request legible copies. If electronic delivery is not practicable, please advise and I will arrange for alternative delivery.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 10 business days.

Sincerely,

Gary Milliette

communications.subject: RE: Wisconsin Open Records Act Request: Lake Geneva Wisconsin Canidates Forum Altercation
communications.communication:
Gary - In response to your open records request dated April 1, 2026, for “Ames/Hoiland altercation, March 18, 2026 – candidates debate,”  I will be forwarding you a link from Axon/Evidence.com, which contains the call detail reports, bodycam videos and audio recordings pertaining to cases 26-4399, 26-4661, 26-4695, 26-4697 and 26-4702. Because the election is next week, I felt forwarding you all the recent complaints concerning city council members promptly would be helpful.

If you would like a more extensive search completed for the items not included in this link, please let me know.  I am also attaching a memo that the Chief put out to our department concerning this matter.

In an effort to protect all persons involved in this case, personally identifiable information as described in Wisconsin State Statute 19.62(5), i.e.: telephone number, street address, VIN and date of birth have been redacted pursuant to § 19.35(1)(am) in all police reports for all subjects. Additionally, the squad computers were blurred as there was information returned from DOT on the screen.  The DPPA (Driver Privacy Protection Act) prohibits a DMV from disclosing records that contain personal information that identifies an individual. This includes: name, address, telephone number, driver identification number, social security number, photographs, and medical or disability information. In an effort to comply with the legal requirements set forth by the DPPA, the following information is redacted by our department prior to any record being released: street and/or mailing address, city, telephone number(s), date of birth, driver’s license number, vehicle identification number, vehicle license plate number, and any medical or disability information. As it pertains to the above listed record, all personal information was obtained from DMV records and therefore, must be redacted to comply with DPPA requirements.

I have also redacted the identity of the anonymous witness in Case 26-4661, and any information that may identify the witness for the following reason: the records custodian must consider the legislative intent to ensure that “all witnesses of crime are treated with dignity, respect, courtesy and sensitivity; and that the rights extended in [Chapter 950] to victims and witnesses of crime are honored and protected by law enforcement agencies, prosecutors and judges.” §950.01, Wis. Stats. Preventing witnesses and victims from being revictimized by disclosure outweighs the public’s right to know their identities.

Additionally, please be advised that the definition of record under the Public Records Law does not include a draft, note, or preliminary form of a document produced by a municipal employee or officer for that individual’s personal use. Wis. Stat. § 19.32(2). Thus, notes kept in a police officer’s memo book are not subject to disclosure pursuant to the Public Records Law if they are considered to be for his or her personal use or to aid his or her recollection. The officers’ notebooks were redacted from body-worn camera video for this reason. I have included the Call Details Report Chronological Order, which details summaries from officers and dispatchers of activities during a police contact. Sometimes these are called “notes”, however, because they are created as a narrative and not solely for the officers’ personal recollection, therefore, those were released.

Additionally, to protect the personal privacy and safety of department members, body-worn camera video was redacted when featuring cell phone, the inside of the police department and the exterior of anonymous witness’ residence.

As I am sure you are aware, if you are not satisfied with this response, you may, pursuant to 19.37(1)(a), Wisconsin Statutes, bring an action for Mandamus asking a court to order the release of the record.  Additionally, you may, pursuant to 19.37(1)(b)m, request the Walworth County District Attorney or the Attorney General to bring an action for Mandamus asking a court to order the release of the record. Should you need anything else, please feel free to contact me.

Sincerely,

Lori Reynolds
Police Records Specialist
Lake Geneva Police Dept.
626 Geneva St.
Lake Geneva, WI 53147
(262) 248-4455

communications.subject: City of Seattle Public Records Request Center - Invoice Ready :: C253153-011626
communications.communication:
Dear Rebecca Turner, 
 
Records in response to your public disclosure request C253153-011626 received on January 16, 2026 are now available. The cost for the records is as follows: 
 
Invoice Number: INV26-C253153-1 
Total: $1.25 
 
To pay online, please follow these steps: 
 Please note that you will need to disable any pop-up blockers to make a payment using the system. 
 
  * Navigate to the Public Records Request Center. 
  * Select 'My Records Request Center'. 
  * Select 'View My Requests'. You may be asked to login. 
  * Find this request: C253153-011626. 
  * Select the 'View Invoices' button. 
 
  * Opens a pop-up window showing your invoice: INV26-C253153-1. 
  * Click on the invoice and then the 'Pay Now' button. 
  * Continue processing your payment. 
  * Select 'Done'. 
  * Once the payment process is done, you are returned to the Public Records Request Center. 
  * After your payment is successful you will receive an email from our payments center. 
Once your payment has been approved, we will send you an email with instructions on how to download your records. Note that City staff must manually upload records. Records will be available one to two business days after payment. 
 
Please let your public disclosure officer (PDO) know if you are unable to pay online; your PDO will work with you on alternative ways to make your payment. 
 
You have 30 days from today's date to pay for your records. If the City does not receive your payment within this time period, your request will be considered abandoned, and you will need to submit a new request. 
 Please add seattle@mycusthelp.net to your E-mail contacts/address book to ensure delivery of the record center E-mails to your Inbox. (mailto:seattle@mycusthelp.net)

RE: Public Disclosure Request Number - C253153-011626 
 
Dear Rebecca Turner:

title: ALPR audit

communications.subject: The Missouri Sunshine Law Request: ALPR audit
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the The Missouri Sunshine Law, I hereby request the following records:

Concerning Flock Safety, these files;

1. Organization Audit in Flock.
The report should include data logged from the period of January 1, 2025, to the date this request is processed. Per Flock's documentation, the Organization Audit is available within the Insights tab and is defined as searches done within the agency.

2. Network Audit in Flock.
The report should include data logged from the period of January 1, 2025, to the date this request is processed. Per Flock's documentation, the Network Audit is available within the Insights tab and is defined as searches of the organization’s Flock network by any agency in the Flock System.

Typically the output for these audits is available in monthly batches.

3. A list of network share settings that include:
3a. Flock networks shared with me
3b. Networks that I’m sharing

Please provide records in their original electronic, machine readable format. For example, please provide csv or spreadsheet files where applicable.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 3 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Traci Phelps

communications.subject: Re: The Missouri Sunshine Law Request: ALPR audit
communications.communication:
Hello,
This correspondence is in response to your Sunshine Law request, reference Sunshine #70 TRACI PHELPS.
After researching the request, the estimated amount of time required to fulfill this request is approximately 4 hours. Pursuant to Section 610.026, RSMo, the cost for staff time is $21.00 per hour, for a total estimated cost of $84.00.
Section 610.026, RSMo, authorizes public governmental bodies to charge for the actual cost of document research, duplication, and staff time required to fulfill a Sunshine Law request.
Please be advised that any records released will be reviewed and may require redaction of information exempt from disclosure under Missouri law, including but not limited to information related to active investigations, confidential records, or other sensitive information as permitted by law.
If you wish to proceed with this request, please remit payment in the amount of $84.00 to our office and reference Sunshine #70 TRACI PHELPS. Upon receipt of payment, processing of your request will begin.
If you choose not to proceed, no further action will be taken. Should you have any questions regarding this estimate, please feel free to contact our office.
Thank you,

[cid:472ac147-2c3b-4017-815e-6e57993528b0]

communications.subject: West Virginia Freedom of Information Act Request: Budget (Madison Police Department)
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the West Virginia Freedom of Information Act, I hereby request the following records:

The itemized budget, sometimes called a Form 700, allocated for your department for the years 2020 through 2025, as well as records sufficient to show any additional funding received through other means, including but not limited to:
• funds for capital expenditures
• grants
• donations

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 5 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Dillon Bergin
