communications.subject: RE: Vermont Public Records Act Request: State of Vermont Governor's Office Return to Office Communications 2025
communications.communication:
Good Afternoon Mr. Galbraith -

In response to your request below, except as set forth herein, attached please find records responsive to your request.

Certain records have been withheld to the extent they are exempt from disclosure under 1 V.S.A. 317(c)(4) as executive privileged. This includes those records advisory to or from the Governor and the Governor's Senior Staff with respect to policy decisions involving the State's Hybrid Work Standard prepared for the purpose of formulating policy and making decisions regarding matters for which the Agency of Administration, members of the Governor's Cabinet, the Tax Department and the Department of Labor were responsible.

You may also be aware that the State has been sued by VSEA over this issue. We are consulting with the Attorney General's Office regarding releasing communications from State employees.

Jaye Pershing Johnson, the Governor's Legal Counsel, is responsible for denial of the privileged and confidential records. I conferred with Ms. Johnson when making the determination to redact or withhold information.

As the records custodian, I certify, as required by 1 V.S.A. 318(a)(2), that I consider the redacted and withheld materials to be exempt from disclosure. You have the right to appeal, as explained in 1 V.S.A. 318, to Jason Gibbs, Chief of Staff, relative to my certification of the records as exempt.

Sincerely,
Karen Pallas
Records Officer

I was unaware at the time of my request legal action was pending, thank you for bringing this to my attention and the explanation as it relates to downstream response actions based on that.

Thank you,
Brandon

communications.communication:

Pursuant to the Washington Public Records Act, I hereby request the following records:

The following metadata for emails sent by all accounts operated by the mayor's office between the dates of January 1, 2026 and the date this request is processed: Sender, Recipient (To, CC, BCC), Date and time sent, Subject. Sincerely,

Rose Terse

communications.subject: City of Seattle Public Records Request Center - Invoice Ready :: C253152-011626
communications.communication:
Dear Rose Terse, 
 
Records in response to your public disclosure request C253152-011626 received on January 16, 2026 are now available. The cost for the records is as follows: 
 
Invoice Number: INV26-C253152-1 
Total: $1.25 
 
To pay online, please follow these steps: 
 Please note that you will need to disable any pop-up blockers to make a payment using the system. 
 
  * Navigate to the Public Records Request Center. 
  * Select 'My Records Request Center'. 
  * Select 'View My Requests'. You may be asked to login. 
  * Find this request: C253152-011626. 
  * Select the 'View Invoices' button. 
 
  * Opens a pop-up window showing your invoice: INV26-C253152-1. 
  * Click on the invoice and then the 'Pay Now' button. 
  * Continue processing your payment. 
  * Select 'Done'. 
  * Once the payment process is done, you are returned to the Public Records Request Center. 
  * After your payment is successful you will receive an email from our payments center. 
Once your payment has been approved, we will send you an email with instructions on how to download your records. Note that City staff must manually upload records. Records will be available one to two business days after payment. 
 
Please let your public disclosure officer (PDO) know if you are unable to pay online; your PDO will work with you on alternative ways to make your payment. 
 
You have 30 days from today's date to pay for your records. If the City does not receive your payment within this time period, your request will be considered abandoned, and you will need to submit a new request. 
 Please add seattle@mycusthelp.net to your E-mail contacts/address book to ensure delivery of the record center E-mails to your Inbox. (mailto:seattle@mycusthelp.net)

Dear Rose Terse, 
Records are now available in response to your request C253152-011626 regarding the following: To Whom It May Concern: 

Pursuant to the Washington Public Records Act, I hereby request the following records: 

The following metadata for emails sent by all accounts operated by the mayor's office between the dates of January 1, 2026 and the date this request is processed: Sender, Recipient (To, CC, BCC), Date and time sent, Subject. As this is a request for metadata, please do not include the email body in the returned records. 

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes. 

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not. 

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 5 business days, as the statute requires. 

Sincerely, 

Rose Terse 

Upload documents directly: https://www.muckrock.com/respond/2179966/ 
The records being made available to you at this time include the following: Metadata 
To download your records, please: 
 
  * Navigate to the Public Records Request Center. 
  * Select 'My Records Request Center'. 
  * Select 'View My Requests'. You may be asked to login. 
  * Select this request: C253152-011626. 
  * Select the 'View Files' button. 
  * You can download records individually or all at the same time by clicking 'Download All'. 
  *  Please save your records as you may only download them from the system three times. If you do not download your records within 30 days of request closure, the link to your records will expire. 
If you believe you have received privileged or confidential records in error, please notify the City of Seattle immediately, through the Public Records Request Center or by email. Privileged or confidential records provided or received in error should not be reviewed, used, disseminated, distributed, or copied, and should be destroyed immediately. Thank you in advance for your cooperation.  We have not prepared a detailed exemption log, as only limited information has been redacted from the responsive records. The information normally provided in an exemption log, such as title, author, recipient, subject, and number of pages, is readily ascertainable from looking at the redacted records. Redactions have been applied throughout the documents pursuant to the following exemption: Ex.01A - Attorney-Client Privileged Communications - RCW 42.56.070 and 5.60.060 - Records reflect communication between a client and an attorney for the purpose of seeking or providing legal advice. 
The Mayor's Office has now provided all responsive records. We consider your request closed and plan to take no further action to fulfill this request. If you have any follow-up questions or would like to initiate an administrative appeal under MDAR 17-0002 Section 6, please contact us within 10 business days after downloading your records, after which this request will no longer be monitored for communications. You may also seek judicial review of our response to your request for a period of one year, beginning as of the date of this message, consistent with RCW 42.56.550. 
Sincerely, 
Tracie Cantrell

communications.subject: New York Freedom of Information Law Request: Israel Bonds (Office of the New York State Comptroller)
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the New York Freedom of Information Law, I hereby request the following records:

Email communications, between October 1st, 2023 and the current date, between leadership at the Office of the Comptroller and anyone from the following organizations:
•	Development Corporation for Israel        @IsraelBonds.com
•	JLens                @JlensNetwork.org
•	Anti-Defamation League      @ADL.org
•	Government of Israel        @gov.il
•	Embassy of Israel Washington     @Washington.mfa.gov.il
•	Consulate General of Israel New-York - @NewYork.mfa.gov.il
Please note, that this is not a keyword search. Instead could you please search based on the email domains listed above? We ask for this time frame to to the popularization of Israel bonds following October 2023.

I understand that some information may be exempt under current law; please provide all non-exempt portions of the documents. The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 5 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Noam Perry
